old_value: {"id":"beads-rw6","title":"Checker: alias-qualified variant lookup can resolve wrong enum owner","description":"In ttrpg_checker/check_expr.rs (around lines 2266-2280 and 2366-2378), alias-qualified variant resolution falls back to owners.first()/matching[0] when disambiguation fails. This can silently bind a variant to an enum outside the aliased system or pick an arbitrary owner when multiple enums share a variant. Expected behavior is deterministic error on ambiguity or no-in-target-owner. Add regression tests for cross-system shared-variant alias access and constructor calls.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T17:19:51Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T17:19:51Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-b60","title":"Unit types: dimensioned numeric types with optional suffix literals","description":"Add unit types to the DSL as described in spec/v0/05_unit_types.ttrpg. A unit type wraps a single int field with type-safe arithmetic and optional literal suffix syntax (e.g., 30ft, 5gp). Declaration: unit TypeName suffix shortname { field: int }. Suffix is optional soft keyword. Lexer emits UnitLiteral tokens (digits+alpha, no space). Checker validates suffix maps to declared unit type. Arithmetic: same-type +/- returns unit, int*unit and unit*int scale, unit/unit returns float, unit/int and unit*unit are errors. Comparisons between same type produce bool, different types error. No implicit conversion to/from int.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:30:00Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:30:00Z"}

new_value: Implemented unit types across all compiler phases: lexer (UnitLiteral token), AST (UnitDecl + UnitLit), parser (unit decl + suffix literal), checker (Ty::UnitType, arithmetic/comparison rules), interpreter (eval + arithmetic ops), CLI (highlighter + types command)

new_value: {"description":"crates/ttrpg_interp/src/lib.rs lines 149-163 evaluate missing action parameter defaults before action::execute_action is called at line 166. If a default expression is effectful (e.g. roll(), apply_condition(), or mechanic calls), those effects run before the ActionStarted event and before veto handling, so a host veto cannot prevent side effects. This is a protocol ordering bug and can leak game-state/effect changes from actions that should have been vetoed."}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-whvi","title":"Dice literals accept zero sides (e.g. 1d0), producing invalid tokens","description":"In crates/ttrpg_lexer/src/lexer.rs, dice sides are parsed at lines 74-77 and used directly without a lower-bound check. Input like 1d0 is tokenized as TokenKind::Dice { count: 1, sides: 0, filter: None } instead of producing a lexer error. This creates invalid DiceExpr values from literals and can crash downstream rollers that assume sides \u003e= 1 (for example random range 1..=sides).\\n\\nFile/lines: crates/ttrpg_lexer/src/lexer.rs:74-77 (parse), :84-88 (token construction).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:14:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:14:58Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-5sg","title":"CLI dice rolling panics on zero-sided DiceExpr","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/effects.rs, both roll helpers call rand::Rng::random_range(1..=expr.sides as i64) without validating expr.sides \u003e= 1 (lines 433 and 468). A DiceExpr with sides=0 reaches this code via DiceLit evaluation and will panic at runtime due to an empty range, crashing CLI execution instead of returning a recoverable runtime error. Repro path: any roll using a DiceExpr with sides=0 and an empty (or exhausted) queue.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:23Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:23Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-2gt","title":"CLI panics on non-ASCII whitespace between command and args","description":"parse_command can panic when the first whitespace after a keyword is multi-byte Unicode (for example U+2003 em space). In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/commands.rs:217-220, split_first_token slices the tail with \u0026s[pos + 1..] after s.find(char::is_whitespace). pos is a byte index, but +1 assumes a 1-byte whitespace character; for multi-byte whitespace this creates a non-char-boundary slice and panics at runtime. Repro input: eval\u003cEM SPACE\u003e2+2 (or any command using Unicode whitespace). This is a process-crash bug in command parsing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:37:46Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:37:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-od7","title":"evaluate_fn_with_values leaks scope on default eval errors","description":"In crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call.rs lines 506-512, evaluate_fn_with_values pushes a scope before evaluating a missing parameter default, then calls eval_expr with ? before env.pop_scope. If default evaluation fails, the function returns early and the pushed scope is never popped. This leaves env.scopes unbalanced and can corrupt later evaluations in the same interpreter session. Repro: define a derive or mechanic default expression that errors, then call evaluate_derive or evaluate_mechanic without that argument.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:50:29Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:50:29Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-5we","title":"clamp_value can panic on invalid bounds and crash interpreter","description":"In crates/ttrpg_interp/src/adapter.rs, clamp_value() uses Rust clamp directly for Int/Float (lines 503-507). i64::clamp/f64::clamp panic when lo \u003e hi, and f64::clamp also panics when either bound is NaN. compute_field_value() calls clamp_value() for bounded mutations (line 390), so malformed or unexpected bounds values can crash the process instead of returning a RuntimeError. This is a production stability bug.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:49:55Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:49:55Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-65cb","title":"Single owner variant patterns skip scrutinee enum validation","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_pattern.rs. In PatternKind::Ident (lines 108-110) and PatternKind::BareDestructure (lines 289-291), owners.len() == 1 immediately resolves the variant owner without checking that scrutinee_ty is Ty::Enum of that owner. The type mismatch diagnostics are only in later branches (lines 110-118 and 291-299), so variant patterns can be accepted against non-enum scrutinee types. This allows invalid programs to type-check and then silently never match at runtime.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:50Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:50Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3av","title":"remove_condition with empty Condition args removes too broadly","description":"crates/ttrpg_interp/src/builtins.rs:316-321 converts Condition args to params=None when args are empty. For RemoveCondition, params=None means remove all same-name conditions, while Some(params) means exact parameter match. This causes remove_condition(target, Condition{name,args:{}}) to over-remove parameterized variants. Condition overload should preserve explicit empty args as Some(empty_map); keep None only for the String overload at lines 325-330.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:55:12Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:55:12Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-b0od","title":"render_diagnostic can panic or OOM on invalid span ordering","description":"In crates/ttrpg_ast/src/diagnostic.rs, span math is unchecked in render_diagnostic (lines 94-95 and 122). local_start = diag.span.start - base_offset / local_end = diag.span.end - base_offset and later local_end - local_start can underflow when a diagnostic has end \u003c start (or when offsets are otherwise inconsistent). In debug builds this can panic; in release it can wrap to a huge usize and drive \"^\".repeat(caret_len) to attempt massive allocation, causing memory blowups/DoS. This is production-impacting because Diagnostic fields are public and spans are not validated at this boundary. Clamp/validate spans before arithmetic (e.g., saturating math + min/max against source bounds).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:16:47Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:16:47Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-hof","title":"CLI allows cross-entity assignments despite typed entity fields","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner.rs, value_matches_ty treats any Value::Entity(_) as compatible with any Ty::Entity(_) at line 2039. That helper is used by spawn field validation (lines 500-517), set validation (lines 730-739), and grant group-field validation (lines 1082-1100). This allows assigning an entity of the wrong concrete type into fields declared as a specific entity type, violating checker compatibility rules and allowing invalid typed references into runtime state.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:38Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:38Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-e4u","title":"resource bound fallback misses identifiers in many expression forms","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval.rs (collect_idents lines 2257-2291, eval_bound_expr lines 2299-2331, resolve_resource_bounds lines 2338-2359). eval_bound_expr retries bound evaluation by collecting identifiers and binding entity fields, but collect_idents does not traverse many expression variants (notably If then/else bodies, IfLet, PatternMatch, GuardMatch, StructLit, etc.). If a bound expression references entity fields inside one of those omitted subtrees, fallback misses required bindings, evaluation fails, and resolve_resource_bounds returns None. That silently drops bounds for MutateField, so clamping is skipped and out-of-range mutations can pass in production.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:47:30Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:47:30Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-srpf","title":"Bare 'some' pattern is parsed as binding, not Option constructor","description":"Bug in crates/ttrpg_parser/src/pattern.rs lines 47 and 93-95: only 'some(...)' is treated as PatternKind::Some; bare 'some' falls through to PatternKind::Ident. In option matches this silently becomes a variable binding that matches every value. Repro: match x { some =\u003e 10, none =\u003e 0 } parses and runs with no diagnostics, and even x=none returns 10. This is a concrete logic bug from a likely typo path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:07:05Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:07:05Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-9h5","title":"Brace newline suppression corrupts token stream when closing brace is missing","description":"In crates/ttrpg_parser/src/parser.rs, suppress_newlines_in_brace_block() (lines 149-175) scans forward to find a matching RBrace, but if no matching brace is found depth never reaches 0 and end becomes tokens.len(). The subsequent removal loop then deletes every Newline token from self.pos to EOF. This mutates the remaining token stream globally and causes downstream parse behavior to diverge (declarations/statements can be merged and recovery quality degrades sharply) after a single missing '}'. The function should bail out without mutation when no matching RBrace exists.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:57:31Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:57:31Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-18k","title":"RemoveCondition override drops params and removes too broadly","description":"In crates/ttrpg_interp/src/adapter.rs, apply_mutation_with_override() handles Effect::RemoveCondition by calling remove_condition(target, name, None) when override_val is Value::Str (lines 345-348). This discards the original params filter from the effect. Production impact: if the original effect intended to remove only a specific parameterized condition, the adapter removes all conditions with that name instead. Expected behavior is to preserve params.as_ref() while replacing only the condition name.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:49:55Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:49:55Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-jgk","title":"Unset float field with -= computes wrong result due Int(0) default + fallback","description":"In crates/ttrpg_interp/src/adapter.rs, compute_field_value() defaults missing field values to Value::Int(0) (line 385). For AssignOp::MinusEq with a float rhs, apply_op() receives (Int, Float) and hits the fallback branch returning rhs unchanged (line 497), rather than performing 0.0 - rhs. Repro at code level: missing field, op -=, rhs=Float(2.5) yields Float(2.5) instead of Float(-2.5). This produces incorrect state mutation semantics for float fields that are first written via -=.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:49:55Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:49:55Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ehjf","title":"Option extends validation is order-dependent in collect pass","description":"In crates/ttrpg_checker/src/collect.rs:1072-1081, collect_option immediately validates o.extends by checking env.options.contains(parent_name). Because options are inserted incrementally during pass_1b, this rejects valid forward references when a child option appears before its parent declaration. The checker then emits a false option extends unknown option error. This conflicts with the later whole-program validation in crates/ttrpg_checker/src/collect.rs:1085-1140 (validate_option_extends), which already runs after all declarations are collected. Impact: valid programs become order-dependent and fail compilation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:04:08Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:04:08Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-01n","title":"Move lowering can make generated calls uncallable via name shadowing","description":"In crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lower.rs, the synthetic mechanic parameters include user receiver/params (lines 159-166), then the body calls roll(...) using a bare identifier (line 171). The synthesized action also calls the synthetic mechanic by bare identifier (line 219). If a move binding is named roll or equals the generated mechanic name, checker name resolution treats the local binding as the callee and reports not callable; valid moves then fail only after lowering. This is non-hygienic lowering of generated call sites. Lowering should use guaranteed-internal identifiers or proactively reject conflicting move binding names.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:00:08Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:00:08Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-bw0","title":"Move lowering captures user result binding in synthesized action","description":"crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lower.rs introduces a hard-coded local binding named result (line 227) and then reads that binding in generated guard arms (line 258). Because move receiver/params are user-controlled and not checked against this name during lowering, user code that uses a binding named result in outcome blocks gets captured by the synthetic binding. This can silently change behavior (for example comparisons now use roll result instead of the move argument) or produce confusing type errors. Lowering should generate a hygienic internal name or reject/conflict-check result in move bindings before synthesis.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:59:59Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:59:59Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-vlw","title":"value_matches_ty accepts arbitrary struct values for TurnBudget/RollResult","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner.rs line 2050, value_matches_ty returns true for (Value::Struct { .. }, Ty::RollResult | Ty::TurnBudget) without checking the struct name. That bypasses nominal typing and allows unrelated struct literals/values to be assigned to TurnBudget or RollResult-typed fields via CLI paths that call this validator (spawn/set/grant). The checker only treats TurnBudget equivalent to struct TurnBudget by name, so the CLI runtime check is currently too permissive and can admit invalid state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:46Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:44:25Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T00:44:25Z","close_reason":"FALSE POSITIVE: CLI-only validation gap, not a language bug — compiled code is properly type-checked"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-4uk","title":"MutateField audit log uses wrong baseline for +=/-= on missing fields","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/effects.rs, MutateField logging reads old via adapter::read_at_path(...).unwrap_or(Value::None) (line 175), but compute_field_value uses Int(0) default for +=/-= when field is absent (adapter behavior). {"id":"tdsl-w3ef","title":"Numeric literal overflow in lexer is silently coerced/wrapped instead of erroring","description":"In crates/ttrpg_lexer/src/lexer.rs, oversized numeric components are silently converted to wrong values: parse failures use unwrap_or(0) and dice count is narrowed with `as u32`.\\n\\nConcrete paths:\\n- line 65: INT parse overflow -\u003e Int(0) (and UnitLiteral value 0 at line 101)\\n- line 77: dice sides parse overflow -\u003e sides = 0\\n- line 134: dice filter amount parse overflow -\u003e n = 0\\n- line 85: dice count cast (i64 -\u003e u32) wraps modulo 2^32 (example: 4294967296d6 becomes count 0)\\n\\nThis masks malformed/overflowing input as valid tokens and changes program behavior without diagnostics.\\n\\nFile/lines: crates/ttrpg_lexer/src/lexer.rs:65,77,85,101,134.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:15:04Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:15:04Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-7rw","title":"ScopeStack::is_group_narrowed leaks optional-group proofs across shadowed bindings","description":"In crates/ttrpg_checker/src/scope.rs lines 181-188, is_group_narrowed scans narrowed_groups across all active scopes and returns true on any matching key, but it does not account for identifier shadowing. If an outer binding/path like actor is narrowed, then an inner scope binds a new actor, checks for the inner actor can incorrectly inherit the outer proof. This can allow optional-group field access or with-constrained calls to type-check without a valid has/with proof for the current binding. Suggested fix: stop lookup when a nearer scope binds the root identifier, or track narrowings by binding identity instead of raw string keys.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:03:56Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:03:56Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-a623","title":"Type resolver ignores user-defined Position/Condition declarations","description":"In crates/ttrpg_checker/src/env.rs, TypeEnv::resolve_type_inner maps TypeExpr::Position and TypeExpr::Condition directly to Ty::Position/Ty::Condition (lines 217-218), while TurnBudget/Duration use resolve_named_or (lines 215-216). Impact: user-declared types named Position or Condition are present in env.types but cannot be referenced by type annotations, causing unreachable declarations and confusing type mismatches.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:05:18Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-rsdi","title":"MultiSourceMap drops file location for legitimate (0,0) spans","description":"In crates/ttrpg_ast/src/diagnostic.rs lines 210-216, MultiSourceMap::render treats (start,end)==(0,0) as a dummy span whenever there are multiple files and returns [severity] message with no filename/line. This conflates dummy spans with real zero-length spans at the start of the first file (e.g., diagnostics at BOF/empty file), causing incorrect/no source attribution in production diagnostics. The heuristic should be replaced with explicit dummy-span signaling or a safer fallback that still attempts file mapping.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:16:51Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:16:51Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-mpj","title":"floor/ceil silently saturate or coerce invalid floats to int","description":"crates/ttrpg_interp/src/builtins.rs:43 and :57 cast f64 to i64 directly after floor()/ceil() using an as-cast. Rust float-to-int casts saturate and map NaN to 0, so out-of-range or non-finite inputs produce silent wrong integers instead of runtime errors (for example ceil(1e309) yields i64::MAX and floor(NaN) yields 0). This bypasses normal overflow/invalid-number protections and can corrupt downstream logic. floor/ceil should validate finite and in-range values before conversion and return RuntimeError when conversion is invalid.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:55:24Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:55:24Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-d01","title":"format_dice_expr overflows/misformats i64::MIN modifier","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/format.rs lines 127-129, negative modifiers are rendered with expr.modifier.abs(). DiceExpr.modifier is i64, so i64::MIN is a valid runtime value. abs() on i64::MIN overflows: with overflow checks enabled this can panic; without checks it stays negative and produces malformed output like 1d20 - -9223372036854775808. This is user-visible CLI corruption and can crash debug builds. Suggested fix: use checked_abs()/unsigned_abs() and special-case i64::MIN so formatting never overflows.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:42:08Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:42:08Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-i0i","title":"CLI command highlighting fails with leading whitespace","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/highlighter.rs, command detection uses split_whitespace() (line 32) so indented commands are recognized as CLI commands, but style application requires token start == 0 (line 64). Because RawLexer skips leading spaces/tabs, the first command token starts at \u003e0 when input is indented (e.g., \"  eval 2+2\"), so it is not highlighted as a command. This is a user-visible REPL highlighting bug. Affected lines: 32-33 and 64 in crates/ttrpg_cli/src/highlighter.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:37:42Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:37:42Z"}

Result: after typing 'assert_err ' users get no suggestions, unlike other assertion commands; this is a functional completion gap for a supported CLI command.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:40:27Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:40:27Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-e4u","title":"resource bound fallback misses identifiers in many expression forms","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval.rs (collect_idents lines 2257-2291, eval_bound_expr lines 2299-2331, resolve_resource_bounds lines 2338-2359). eval_bound_expr retries bound evaluation by collecting identifiers and binding entity fields, but collect_idents does not traverse many expression variants (notably If then/else bodies, IfLet, PatternMatch, GuardMatch, StructLit, etc.). If a bound expression references entity fields inside one of those omitted subtrees, fallback misses required bindings, evaluation fails, and resolve_resource_bounds returns None. That silently drops bounds for MutateField, so clamping is skipped and out-of-range mutations can pass in production.","notes":"Triage: real but low practical risk — collect_idents misses some expression forms but resource bounds are almost always simple expressions like 0..=max_HP.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:47:30Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:37:42Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-cd7","title":"Struct literal base-spread form rejects trailing comma","description":"Bug in crates/ttrpg_parser/src/expr.rs around parse_struct_lit (lines 402-405 and 428). The leading DotDot base branch parses DotDot expr but never consumes an optional trailing comma before }. Repro: ttrpg_parser::parse_expr(\"Point { ..other, }\") fails with an unexpected comma before }. Expected: parse succeeds, consistent with existing support for trailing comma after DotDot base when fields are present (\"Point { x: 1, ..defaults, }\"). Actual: parser errors because it immediately expects RBrace.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:01:20Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:01:20Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-jcu","title":"ScopeStack::bind preserves stale narrowed_groups on same-scope rebind","description":"In crates/ttrpg_checker/src/scope.rs lines 98-101, bind replaces bindings[name] but does not clear any existing narrowed_groups entry for that same key (see lines 169-176). If a name is rebound in the same scope after a narrowing was recorded, the new binding inherits stale optional-group proofs from the previous binding. This can suppress required checker diagnostics for optional-group access and with constraints. Suggested fix: when binding a name, remove narrowed_groups[name], or migrate narrowing state to binding IDs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:03:59Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:03:59Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-t4c","title":"Synthetic mechanic collision check misses table/hook declarations","description":"crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lower.rs builds existing_names from Derive/Mechanic/Action/Reaction/Prompt only (lines 35-44). Hook and Table declarations are omitted even though they are collected in the callable function namespace during checking, so synthetic mechanic names are not collision-checked against those declarations. This produces inconsistent diagnostics and allows lowering to proceed into duplicate-function errors later instead of reporting the intended synthetic-name collision at lowering time. Include Hook and Table names in the pre-lowering collision set.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:00:18Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:00:18Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-rw0","title":"Qualified types in modify let annotations are not desugared","description":"In crates/ttrpg_parser/src/resolve.rs, desugar_decl_types only walks signature/field type positions and skips modify-clause let annotations. Condition handling at lines 449-454 desugars receiver/params but does not traverse c.clauses; Option declarations are fully skipped at line 480. As a result, TypeExpr::Qualified inside ModifyStmt::Let type annotations (reachable from condition modify blocks and option when_enabled modifies) is left unresolved and later rejected by checker validation as requiring module resolution. Resolver should recurse into modify bodies and desugar those type annotations too.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:54:50Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:54:50Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ky7v","title":"Checker allows direct turn reassignment that runtime rejects","description":"In crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_stmt.rs, the direct assignment branch in check_assign (lines 96-108) only checks mutable binding and does not forbid assigning to the implicit turn binding. Because turn is mutable, turn = ... type-checks. This conflicts with runtime behavior, which rejects direct turn reassignment and only permits field mutation (for example turn.actions -= 1). The mismatch causes a late runtime error instead of a checker error.\\n\\nFile references:\\n- crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_stmt.rs:96-108\\n- crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_stmt.rs:147-149","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:12:17Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:12:17Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-nmfv","title":"Variant ambiguity check ignores system visibility","description":"File crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check.rs lines 73-99: resolve_bare_variant_with_hint decides ambiguity from env.variant_to_enums without filtering by current_system visibility. In module-aware mode, a bare variant can be reported ambiguous because of unimported systems; repro: A defines variant short, B defines short, C imports only A, C still gets ambiguous short. Expected: consider only visible owners for the current system before emitting ambiguity.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:11:32Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:11:32Z"}

If ty is a declared non-enum type (for example struct or entity), the checker emits an incorrect undefined type error instead of reporting that the type exists but is not an enum.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:53Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:53Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ysqd","title":"Typed let in modify body skips type visibility checks","description":"File crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_modify.rs. In ModifyStmt::Let handling (lines 159-162), checker runs validate_type and resolve_type for the annotation but does not run check_type_visible. The regular let checker in crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_stmt.rs does call check_type_visible (lines 30-32). Concrete impact: in module-aware checking, a typed let inside a modify body can reference a type from another system without required imports and no visibility diagnostic is emitted, unlike equivalent let statements elsewhere.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:35Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:35Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-p0bd","title":"Suppress clauses skip receiver with-group validation and narrowing","description":"File crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_modify.rs. In check_modify_clause (lines 53-69), receiver with-groups are validated and narrowed via validate_with_groups. In check_suppress_clause (lines 271-356), receiver is bound (lines 282-289) but validate_with_groups is never called and receiver_with_groups is not passed in. Concrete impact: invalid receiver with-group names can pass with no diagnostic when a condition only has suppress clauses, and suppress binding expressions can incorrectly fail optional-group access checks because narrowing for declared with-groups is missing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:30Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:09:30Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-1cyf","title":"concat second list is checked without expected list type hint","description":"Bug in crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_expr.rs lines 1590-1599: check_concat_call type-checks second argument via check_expr with no hint, even after first list type is known. This can reject valid code when the second list contains ambiguous bare enum variants that should be disambiguated by the first list element type. Repro shape: concat(list\u003cDamageType\u003e, [fire]) where fire is shared by multiple enums should resolve using list\u003cDamageType\u003e hint but currently reports ambiguity.","notes":"Lead issue for type-hint propagation batch. See also: tdsl-qkkn, tdsl-6ut3, tdsl-1qvb","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:04:31Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:22Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-6fad","title":"Duplicate type declarations across systems can overwrite first type body","description":"In crates/ttrpg_checker/src/collect.rs:58-62, collect_inner calls pass_1b separately for each system. Inside pass_1b (crates/ttrpg_checker/src/collect.rs:252), processed_types is local to one call. If two systems declare the same type name, pass_1a reports a duplicate (lines 228-239) and keeps the first stub, but the second system declaration is still processed in a later pass_1b call and writes into the same existing entry via get_mut in collectors (for example lines 431-433, 458-460, 541-544, 619-622). Impact: a duplicate declaration that should be ignored can overwrite the canonical type fields or variants, causing inconsistent downstream type checking.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:04:08Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:04:08Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-99qw","title":"Pattern parser rejects -N integer patterns","description":"Bug in crates/ttrpg_parser/src/pattern.rs lines 10-19 and 99-101: parse_pattern handles Int(n) but not a leading Minus token. Pattern '-1' is rejected as expected pattern found Minus, so negative literals cannot be used in match/if let/for patterns. Repro: match x { -1 =\u003e 10, _ =\u003e 0 }.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:07:01Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:07:01Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-v13","title":"Qualified type parsing breaks for aliases matching soft type keywords","description":"crates/ttrpg_parser/src/types.rs: built-in/soft type branches (lines 12-74) run before qualified-type parsing (lines 78-83). If an alias matches one of those names (example: use \"other\" as Duration), a type like Duration.SomeType is parsed via the Duration built-in arm (line 20) instead of TypeExpr::Qualified, leaving .SomeType unconsumed and producing parse errors. This makes valid module-qualified type references unparsable when alias names collide with soft keywords like Duration/option/map/list/set/resource.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:58:31Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:58:31Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-q2p","title":"recover_to_decl misses hook/table/unit declaration starts","description":"In crates/ttrpg_parser/src/parser.rs, is_decl_start() (lines 294-303) omits valid declaration keywords that parse_decl accepts (hook, table, unit). During error recovery, recover_to_decl() (lines 278-292) uses is_decl_start() at depth 0 to decide where to stop. If a parse error occurs before a valid hook/table/unit declaration, recovery skips past that declaration instead of resynchronizing on it, causing concrete lost declarations and cascading diagnostics. Fix by including all parse_decl declaration starters in is_decl_start().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:57:31Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:57:31Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-6me","title":"CLI validator can miss unclosed delimiters after stray closer","description":"crates/ttrpg_cli/src/validator.rs:21,39,54 uses one signed depth counter for all delimiter types and decrements on any closer, then only checks depth\u003e0. Repro: input ') {' produces depth -1 then 0, so validator returns Complete even though '{' is unclosed; REPL executes prematurely instead of prompting continuation for multiline input.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:44:06Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:44:06Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-t3c","title":"format_value emits unescaped strings in CLI output","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/format.rs line 11, Value::Str is formatted with a raw interpolation inside quotes: format!(\"\\\"{}\\\"\", s). Embedded quotes, backslashes, and control characters are not escaped. Example: Value::Str(\"a\\\"b\") renders as \"a\"b\" (ambiguous/unbalanced), and Value::Str(\"x\\ny\") emits a literal newline into output. This also affects path rendering at lines 105-108 because format_path delegates index formatting to format_value, so indexed string keys can produce malformed paths/log lines. Suggested fix: use escaped string-literal formatting (for example debug-style escaping) for Value::Str.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:42:14Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:42:14Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-1k4","title":"Completer uses wrong replacement span for spawn/do/call after extra input","description":"crates/ttrpg_cli/src/completer.rs has cursor/span bugs: spawn at lines 102-103 computes current via split_first_word(rest) and sets word_start=pos-current.len(), so after additional args (e.g. spawn Character hero) completion targets/replaces the wrong region; do at lines 111-113 and call at lines 121-123 compute current from text before '(' then still use pos-current.len(), so tab after '(' or inside args can overwrite unrelated text. Impact: corrupted input edits during tab completion.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:40:21Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:40:21Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-24qg","title":"Variant visibility error can point to wrong owner system","description":"File crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check.rs lines 126-150: check_name_visible uses env.variant_owner.get(name) for Namespace::Variant, but variant_owner is a single-owner map keyed by bare variant name, so duplicate variant names across systems overwrite each other. When visibility fails, the diagnostic can suggest importing the wrong system because owner is arbitrary/last-write rather than the resolved enum owner or all possible owners.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:11:35Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T00:11:35Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-vpk","title":"parse_multi reparses every source and retains unused ASTs","description":"In crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lib.rs, parse_multi performs a full parse/lower pass in the first loop (lines 83-118), then clears diagnostic/module-prep state and reparses all sources again in a second loop (lines 135-177). The first-pass results are not used in output: file_programs and base_offsets are populated (lines 79-82) but the merge path uses rebased_programs only (lines 179-184). This doubles parse/lower CPU cost and keeps first-pass Program values resident until function return, increasing peak memory for large multi-file inputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:54:19Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:54:19Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3zv","title":"Reload/load path tokenization breaks files with spaces","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner.rs, cmd_load tokenizes input with split_whitespace at line 264, and cmd_reload reconstructs paths by joining last_paths with spaces at lines 402-408 before re-calling cmd_load. Any path containing whitespace is split into multiple tokens, so reload can target the wrong files or fail with cannot read errors. This is a concrete production failure for valid filesystem paths with spaces.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:42Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:42Z"}

new_value: Not a bug: two-layer arity protection (checker + bind_args) already prevents wrong arity reaching builtins
Not reachable: pos bounded by token count, offsets are small constants (1-3), overflow impossible
Not reachable: all callers use monotonic cursor positions, invariant maintained by lexer
Not reachable: bind_args guarantees length matches fn_info.params, both from same source
Not reachable: all lexer/parser paths produce valid spans with start <= end
Not reachable: all push/pop pairs verified balanced across all callers. Vec::pop on empty is safe (returns None)

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-z6xa","title":"crates/ttrpg_parser/src/pattern.rs: handle overflow in negative integer pattern parsing","description":"parse_pattern negates integer tokens directly when parsing minus-prefixed literals. If the value is the minimum signed integer, unary negation can overflow. Use checked negation and emit a parser diagnostic on overflow (or parse signed literals earlier) to make behavior explicit and safe.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:29:02Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:29:02Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-36qk","title":"crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call.rs: harden builtin/method arity checks to avoid panics","description":"Several call helpers index args directly (e.g. eval_ordinal, eval_from_ordinal, eval_try_from_ordinal, collection builtins, and eval_option_method via args[0]/args[1]). If checker invariants are bypassed or stale, interpreter panics with index out of bounds instead of returning RuntimeError. Add centralized arity validation for these entry points and return span-aware runtime errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:23:40Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:23:40Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-0s0y","title":"crates/ttrpg_interp/src/builtins.rs: guard floor/ceil against non-finite and out-of-range floats","description":"builtin_floor and builtin_ceil convert Float to Int via f.floor() as i64 / f.ceil() as i64. Casting float to int with as can silently saturate or coerce edge cases (NaN, +/-inf, very large magnitudes), producing values that may hide invalid input. Add explicit finite/range checks and return runtime errors for invalid conversions, with tests covering NaN/infinity/out-of-range values.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:23:30Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:23:30Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-tsn2","title":"crates/ttrpg_cli/src/format.rs: avoid overflow when formatting negative dice modifier","description":"format_dice_expr uses expr.modifier.abs() for negative values. For signed integer minimum (e.g., i32::MIN), abs can overflow/panic in debug or produce incorrect output in release. Refactor to use a non-overflowing path (e.g., unsigned_abs/saturating_abs or wider type conversion) and add a regression test for the min-value edge case.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:22:11Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:22:11Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-9fm8","title":"crates/ttrpg_ast/src/diagnostic.rs: Resolve end_exclusive boundary contract in MultiSourceMap","description":"FileEntry.end_exclusive is documented as a half-open upper bound (base_offset + source.len()), but find_file matches with span_start \u003c= end_exclusive. This mixes inclusive and exclusive semantics and can treat the sentinel-gap byte as file-owned. Align the contract and implementation (or rename fields), then add tests around EOF/sentinel boundaries across adjacent files.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:44Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:44Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-li9m","title":"crates/ttrpg_ast/src/diagnostic.rs: Harden diagnostic span normalization in renderer","description":"render_diagnostic derives local_start/local_end, line indexing, and caret/space repeat widths directly from raw span offsets. Out-of-range or malformed spans can trigger usize underflow/panic or very large repeat allocations. Add a normalization step that validates/clamps spans to file bounds, handles reversed spans safely, and caps rendered underline/indent lengths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:44Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:18:44Z"}

new_value: Fixed: uses checked_neg now. Note: lexer prevents the overflow case (rejects literals > i64::MAX), so this was defensive hardening rather than a live bug.
Fixed: all collection builtins and ordinal/from_ordinal/try_from_ordinal/unwrap_or now check arity before indexing args. CLI eval no longer panics on wrong arity.
Fixed: floor/ceil now reject NaN, infinity, and out-of-range floats with runtime errors instead of silently producing wrong values.
Fixed: format_dice_expr uses (modifier as i128).unsigned_abs() to safely handle i64::MIN without panic.
Fixed: changed find_file boundary check from <= to < (exclusive), preventing wrong file attribution at sentinel gaps.
Fixed: replaced subtraction with saturating_sub to prevent underflow on inverted spans.

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-cd7","title":"Struct literal base-spread form rejects trailing comma","description":"Bug in crates/ttrpg_parser/src/expr.rs around parse_struct_lit (lines 402-405 and 428). The leading DotDot base branch parses DotDot expr but never consumes an optional trailing comma before }. Repro: ttrpg_parser::parse_expr(\"Point { ..other, }\") fails with an unexpected comma before }. Expected: parse succeeds, consistent with existing support for trailing comma after DotDot base when fields are present (\"Point { x: 1, ..defaults, }\"). Actual: parser errors because it immediately expects RBrace.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:01:20Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-uid","title":"assert_err has no argument completion despite being a supported command","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/completer.rs, assert_err is listed in ALL_COMMANDS at line 12 but is missing from the completion match arms (expression completion at lines 186-201 only handles eval/assert/assert_eq). Result: after typing 'assert_err ' users get no suggestions, unlike other assertion commands; this is a functional completion gap for a supported CLI command.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:40:27Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-d51v","title":"crates/ttrpg_cli/src/highlighter.rs: Fix CLI command highlighting with leading whitespace","description":"Highlighter determines CLI command via first non-whitespace word, but applies command style only when token span starts at index 0. This makes indented commands (e.g., '  eval 2+2') lose command highlighting. Refactor first-token detection to align with lexer spans (or track first non-whitespace token) and add regression tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:22:08Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:22:08Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-i0i","title":"CLI command highlighting fails with leading whitespace","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/highlighter.rs, command detection uses split_whitespace() (line 32) so indented commands are recognized as CLI commands, but style application requires token start == 0 (line 64). Because RawLexer skips leading spaces/tabs, the first command token starts at \u003e0 when input is indented (e.g., \"  eval 2+2\"), so it is not highlighted as a command. This is a user-visible REPL highlighting bug. Affected lines: 32-33 and 64 in crates/ttrpg_cli/src/highlighter.rs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:37:42Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-d01","title":"format_dice_expr overflows/misformats i64::MIN modifier","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/format.rs lines 127-129, negative modifiers are rendered with expr.modifier.abs(). DiceExpr.modifier is i64, so i64::MIN is a valid runtime value. abs() on i64::MIN overflows: with overflow checks enabled this can panic; without checks it stays negative and produces malformed output like 1d20 - -9223372036854775808. This is user-visible CLI corruption and can crash debug builds. Suggested fix: use checked_abs()/unsigned_abs() and special-case i64::MIN so formatting never overflows.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:42:08Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-6ut3","title":"append element argument ignores list element type hint","description":"Bug in crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_expr.rs lines 1547-1555: check_append_call checks the second argument with check_expr before validating against list element type. {"id":"tdsl-vlw","title":"value_matches_ty accepts arbitrary struct values for TurnBudget/RollResult","description":"In crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner.rs line 2050, value_matches_ty returns true for (Value::Struct { .. }, Ty::RollResult | Ty::TurnBudget) without checking the struct name. That bypasses nominal typing and allows unrelated struct literals/values to be assigned to TurnBudget or RollResult-typed fields via CLI paths that call this validator (spawn/set/grant). The checker only treats TurnBudget equivalent to struct TurnBudget by name, so the CLI runtime check is currently too permissive and can admit invalid state.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:46Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-t4c","title":"Synthetic mechanic collision check misses table/hook declarations","description":"crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lower.rs builds existing_names from Derive/Mechanic/Action/Reaction/Prompt only (lines 35-44). Hook and Table declarations are omitted even though they are collected in the callable function namespace during checking, so synthetic mechanic names are not collision-checked against those declarations. This produces inconsistent diagnostics and allows lowering to proceed into duplicate-function errors later instead of reporting the intended synthetic-name collision at lowering time. Include Hook and Table names in the pre-lowering collision set.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T00:00:18Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

This allows assigning an entity of the wrong concrete type into fields declared as a specific entity type, violating checker compatibility rules and allowing invalid typed references into runtime state.","notes":"Triage: real but low severity — CLI-only, compiled code is properly checked. value_matches_ty accepts any entity for any entity type.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:38:38Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

Resolver should recurse into modify bodies and desugar those type annotations too.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:54:50Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-e4u","title":"resource bound fallback misses identifiers in many expression forms","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval.rs (collect_idents lines 2257-2291, eval_bound_expr lines 2299-2331, resolve_resource_bounds lines 2338-2359). eval_bound_expr retries bound evaluation by collecting identifiers and binding entity fields, but collect_idents does not traverse many expression variants (notably If then/else bodies, IfLet, PatternMatch, GuardMatch, StructLit, etc.). If a bound expression references entity fields inside one of those omitted subtrees, fallback misses required bindings, evaluation fails, and resolve_resource_bounds returns None. That silently drops bounds for MutateField, so clamping is skipped and out-of-range mutations can pass in production.","notes":"Triage: real but low practical risk — collect_idents misses some expression forms but resource bounds are almost always simple expressions like 0..=max_HP.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:47:30Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:24:16Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-gdqe","title":"Method-call syntax for actions: entity.Action(args)","description":"Support method-call syntax for action invocation. Currently actions are called as functions with the receiver as the first argument: Attack(attacker, target, weapon). This adds a method-call form: attacker.Attack(target, weapon), which desugars to the function-call form.

## Motivation
- Natural readability: 'attacker.Attack(target, weapon)' reads as subject-verb-object
- Consistency with existing method syntax on collections, options, dice, strings
- Actions already have an explicit receiver via 'on receiver: Type' — method syntax is the natural complement
- PascalCase action names visually distinguish from lowercase builtin methods

## Design (spec updated in 03_canonical_grammar.ttrpg)
- Both forms are equivalent: method form desugars to function form
- Resolution order for obj.Name(args): enum constructor > module alias > action dispatch > builtin method > error
- Action methods checked before builtin methods (PascalCase vs lowercase avoids collision)
- Context restrictions still apply (action/reaction/hook resolve blocks only)
- Named arguments work in both forms

## Implementation plan

### Checker (check_expr.rs)
1. In check_method_call(), before the catch-all error arm, add an action lookup:
   - Use env.lookup_fn(method) to find a FnInfo with kind == FnKind::Action
   - Verify the action's receiver type is compatible with obj_ty
   - Check context restrictions (must be in action/reaction/hook resolve block)
   - Build effective_params WITHOUT receiver (obj already fills that role)
   - Type-check remaining args against action params
   - Return the action's return type (unit)
2. Alternatively, intercept in the FieldAccess branch of check_call() before calling check_method_call()

### Interpreter (call.rs)
1. In eval_method_call(), before the catch-all error arm, add action dispatch:
   - Check if method name is an action in the program
   - The object value must be Value::Entity
   - Construct the full argument list with object as receiver, then remaining args
   - Call dispatch_action() with the synthetic argument list
2. Alternatively, intercept in the FieldAccess branch of eval_call() before calling eval_method_call()

### Tests
- Basic method syntax: entity.Action(args) works
- Equivalence: both forms produce the same result
- Named args: entity.Action(target: x, weapon: y)
- Error: method syntax on non-entity type
- Error: action name not matching receiver type
- Error: calling in wrong context (derive, etc.)
- No collision with builtin methods (different naming conventions)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T18:16:21Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-25T18:16:21Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-d1i7","title":"Add alias bindings for optional groups at narrowing sites","description":"Allow 'as <alias>' syntax at the two points where optional group existence is proven:

1. **`with` constraints**: `action CastSpell on caster: Character with AdvancedSpellcasting as sc (cost: int)` — alias scoped to the function body
2. **`has` guards**: `if caster has AdvancedSpellcasting as sc { caster.sc.spell_slots -= cost }` — alias scoped to the guard block

This lets users avoid repeating long group names in field access paths. The alias is always locally scoped (no global renaming), and only valid where narrowing has been proven.

Touches: parser (parse 'as ident' after group name in with/has), AST (store optional alias on WithConstraint and HasExpr), checker (register alias in scope for field resolution), interpreter (resolve alias during field access).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T14:45:44Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-27T14:45:44Z"}

{"design":"## Design: Tags & Signature Selectors for Modify Targets

### Tag Declarations

New top-level declaration:

```
tag attack
tag saving_throw
```

Tags applied inline after function signature (before body):

```
mechanic attack_roll(...) -> AttackResult #attack #ranged { ... }
mechanic spell_attack(...) -> AttackResult #attack #ranged { ... }
mechanic saving_throw(...) -> SaveResult #saving_throw { ... }
```

Multiple tags allowed per function. Checker validates:
- Tags on functions must be declared
- Tags in selectors must be declared
- Warn on declared-but-unused tags

### Selector Syntax

Modify clauses accept a bracketed selector instead of a function name:

```
modify [#attack](attacker: bearer) {
    mode = disadvantage
}

modify [returns AttackResult, has attacker: Character](attacker: bearer) {
    mode = disadvantage
}

modify [#attack, has attacker: Character](attacker: bearer) {
    mode = disadvantage
}
```

Selector predicates (AND-only, no OR):

| Predicate            | Matches                                      |
|----------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| `#tag`               | Function has the given tag                    |
| `returns T`          | Return type is T                              |
| `has param: Type`    | Has parameter named `param` of type `Type`    |
| `has param`          | Has parameter named `param` (any type)        |

### Checker: Universal Validation

Treat selector-targeted modify as "for all matched mechanics":

1. Resolve selector to match set M (only derives/mechanics, same as today)
2. If M is empty → warning
3. For each binding in header (e.g. `attacker: bearer`): require every m in M to have parameter `attacker`
4. Require that parameter type to be identical across all m in M (exact match for v1)
5. For each mutation in body (e.g. `mode = disadvantage`): require every return type in M to have `mode` with compatible type
6. Any violation → compile error with per-mechanic diagnostics

No partial application, no silent skips.

### Existing name-based targeting unchanged

```
modify attack_roll(attacker: bearer) { ... }   // still works
```

### Future: Tag Contracts (not in v1)

```
tag attack requires [has attacker: Character, returns AttackResult]
```

Prevents tagging drift — the checker verifies tagged functions conform to the contract.

### Scope

Only derives and mechanics can be tagged (same restriction as modify targets today). Tags on actions/reactions/hooks/prompts are a checker error."}

If a new attack mechanic is added, existing conditions silently miss it. Consider a grouping mechanism (e.g. a `trait` or tag on mechanics) so conditions can target all functions in a group. Example: `modify [attack](attacker: bearer)` could target all mechanics tagged as attack resolution, rather than listing resolve_melee_attack and resolve_missile_attack separately.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:43:53Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:43:53Z"}

{"notes":"## Analysis (2026-02-28)

### Current State
Duration is a ruleset-customizable enum — rulesets can already add variants like `until_next_use` and `until_save`. The interpreter is fully opaque to Duration semantics (stores value on ActiveCondition, passes through Effect::ApplyCondition). So the variants can exist today, but the host bears all burden of knowing when to remove them.

What's missing is any DSL-level mechanism for a condition to express 'I expire when X happens.'

### Could emit/events solve this?
The emit/hook system fires hooks inline and synchronously, and hooks can call remove_condition(). Conceptually:

```
condition Helped(source: Character) on bearer: Character {
    modify attack_roll + 1d4 {
        emit ConditionUsed(condition: self, bearer: bearer)
    }
}
on ConditionUsed(condition, bearer) {
    remove_condition(bearer, condition)
}
```

**Problems with this approach:**
1. No `self` reference in conditions — modify blocks can't reference their own identity for removal
2. Modify blocks can't emit — they're expression-level (pure), not statement-level
3. Ordering risk — removal during modifier evaluation could invalidate the modifier stack mid-resolution
4. `until_save` doesn't map cleanly — no modifier-fires event for saves; it's host-driven end-of-turn logic

### Possible Directions
- **Duration clause on conditions** referencing events: `duration: until ConditionUsed`
- **Host-interpreted variants** (status quo — add enum variants, host handles lifecycle)
- **Hybrid** where DSL expresses intent (`until_next_use`) and runtime has built-in support for consuming conditions after modifier application

Emit works conceptually for `until_next_use` with extensions but is awkward for `until_save`. No decision made yet."}

{"design":"## Design Direction

**Core insight**: Spatial operations are always host-resolved. The DSL expresses spatial *intent*; the host decides how to fulfill it — geometry for VTTs, prompting the GM for table-helper apps, or whatever else.

### Why host-resolved?

The host may be a helper app for people playing at a real table with physical minis. It has no position data — it can't compute direction vectors or search for nearby entities. Only the humans looking at the table know spatial relationships. A VTT host has authoritative grid/hex data and can compute everything. The DSL must work for both.

### Existing pattern

`distance(a, b)` already follows this model — it's host-delegated via `StateProvider`. Spatial operations are more of the same mechanism, not a new concept.

### What the DSL needs to express

The three pain points map to two categories:

**Spatial effects** (mutations — tell the host to change the world):
- Shove: `move(target, away_from: actor, distance: 5)` — host repositions the token however it can

**Spatial queries** (predicates/collections — ask the host about the world):
- Flanking: `query flanking(attacker, ally, target) -> bool` — VTT does geometry, table helper asks the GM
- AoE: `query creatures_within(center, radius) -> list<Entity>` — VTT filters by distance, table helper asks GM to select targets

### Consequences

- Spatial operations are **not pure** — they cannot appear in `derive` blocks (correct: you wouldn't derive a field from 'ask the GM if they're flanking')
- They live in the same category as effects, not builtins
- The `StateProvider` trait surface stays lean — spatial resolution goes through effect dispatch

### Future path

1. Add spatial effects for mutations (move/reposition)
2. Add spatial queries for predicates and collections
3. Ship an optional **geometry helper library** that VTT hosts can plug in to auto-resolve spatial queries against grid/hex data (Chebyshev, hex distance, line-of-sight, etc.) so they don't each reimplement the math"}

{"id":"tdsl-586","title":"Language: position arithmetic and spatial queries","description":"Position is opaque — only `distance(a, b) -> int` is available. Can't express:
- 'Move target 5 feet away from shover' (Shove action)
- 'Is attacker on the opposite side of target from ally?' (Flanking)
- 'All creatures within 20 feet of a point' (AoE targeting)
- Direction vectors, line-of-sight, adjacency

Would need builtins like:
    direction(from: Position, to: Position) -> Direction
    move_toward(pos: Position, target: Position, distance: int) -> Position
    move_away(pos: Position, source: Position, distance: int) -> Position
    creatures_within(center: Position, radius: int) -> list<Character>","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T15:51:59Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-02-24T16:45:53Z","work_type":"mutex"}

new_value: Resolved by design: explicit emit + mechanic wrappers (e.g. deal_damage) is the right pattern. Auto-emission from field mutations is too implicit and creates footguns (invisible cascades, infinite loops). Future privacy system will restrict direct field writes, making the mechanic-wrapper pattern compiler-enforced rather than convention-based.

Not needed. Use cases are already covered: resource() handles clamped values, enums are better for small discrete sets (spell slot levels, proficiency tiers), and requires guards validate at call sites. Adding bounded int types introduces type system complexity (subtyping, arithmetic on bounds) that veers into dependent typing for minimal practical gain.

Implemented until_next_use and until_save duration patterns in expanded example. Added ConsumeOnUse (modify_applied) and UntilSaveCheck (turn_end + conditions()) hooks. Fixed checker to support Ty::Duration in pattern matching and variable bindings in enum destructuring sub-patterns.

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-v20v","title":"Pattern binding fails for enum-typed scrutinees in match/for","description":"The spec says bare IDENT in pattern position should be a variable binding if it doesn't match an enum variant in scope. Currently, the checker rejects non-variant identifiers with 'unknown identifier X in match on enum Y; if this is meant as a catch-all, use _'. This affects both match arms (e.g. 'other =\u003e 0') and for-loop patterns (e.g. 'for c in colors'). The for-loop case is especially impactful since there's no workaround — you can't iterate over an enum-typed list and bind each element to a variable. Root cause is likely in the checker's pattern resolution logic which treats all bare idents as attempted variant matches when the scrutinee/element type is an enum, rather than falling back to binding.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T16:14:16Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T16:14:16Z"}

new_value: Fixed: bare idents in pattern position now fall through to variable binding when not a known variant, regardless of enum scrutinee type

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-i86k","title":"check -c does not support stdin/pipe input","description":"When using 'check -c', the next argument is taken as the literal source string. There's no way to pipe source into check via stdin. Attempting 'cat file | ttrpg check -c -' treats '-' as the literal source text (a minus sign), not as a stdin indicator. This makes it awkward to use check in shell pipelines or with heredocs. The run command supports pipe mode (non-terminal stdin), but check -c has no equivalent. Either: (1) make 'check -c -' read from stdin, (2) make 'check' without arguments read from stdin when it's a pipe (like run does), or (3) both.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T16:17:04Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T16:17:04Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-cp5j","title":"No map literal syntax in DSL expressions","description":"The spec grammar has no map_lit production in primary_expr.
