old_value:
{"id":"tdsl-ljhg","title":"Bug: list.sum() uses unchecked i64 accumulation","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/methods.rs

Why this is a bug: In eval_list_method, the sum() path adds integers with int_sum += n without checked arithmetic. For large valid int lists this can overflow i64. In debug builds this panics; in release it can wrap to incorrect values, violating the interpreters overflow error semantics used by other integer arithmetic operations.

Potential impact: Calling .sum() on large integer lists can crash the interpreter or return silently incorrect totals.

Suggested fix: Replace unchecked integer addition with checked_add and return RuntimeError when overflow occurs, consistent with other numeric operations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:05:02Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:05:02Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-0vyg","title":"Bug: Nested-entity assignment skips lvalue alias rewrite","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval/assign.rs

Why this is a bug: eval_assign_local detects when a local path reaches an Entity and emits MutateField, but unlike eval_assign_entity it never applies resolved_lvalue_aliases from the checker. For valid lvalues that use a group alias on the entity segment, runtime mutation uses the alias token instead of the real group name.

Potential impact: Assignments through local containers (for example wrapper.entity.alias.field -= 1) can mutate the wrong path or become a silent no-op when the alias field does not exist in state, despite passing type checking.

Suggested fix: In the entity branch inside eval_assign_local, apply the same alias rewrite as eval_assign_entity, adjusting the stored segment index by depth before rewriting the emitted path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:07:15Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:07:15Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ldmi","title":"Bug: Cost token alias resolution can shadow exact field names","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/env.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: resolve_cost_token applies legacy aliases (e.g., action -\u003e actions) before checking exact field-name matches. If a ruleset defines both singular and plural fields (for example, action and actions), cost token 'action' resolves to the alias target 'actions', making the exact 'action' field unreachable in cost clauses.\\n\\nPotential impact: Valid schemas with both singular/plural resource names can have costs bound to the wrong field, causing incorrect type checking and runtime behavior.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Prefer exact field-name matches before applying legacy alias remapping, or only apply aliases when no exact field exists.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T02:07:57Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:07:57Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-0r4g","title":"Bug: Dotted-path group narrowing ignores root shadowing","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/scope.rs

Why this is a bug: ScopeStack::is_group_narrowed and ScopeStack::resolve_group_alias stop scope-walk when scope.bindings.contains_key(var) is true, but var may be a dotted path key such as trigger.actor produced by extract_path_key. Bindings store only root identifiers such as trigger, so root shadowing is not detected for dotted keys. This can make inner scopes incorrectly inherit outer narrowings and aliases for a different shadowed variable.

Potential impact: The checker can accept optional-group access or alias-based group access in inner scopes when it should reject them, causing incorrect type-checking and unsound behavior around has-based narrowing.

Suggested fix: In both methods, derive the root identifier from var (substring before first dot) and stop when either the full key or that root is bound in the current scope. This preserves correct shadowing semantics for both simple and dotted path keys.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:33:48Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:33:48Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3947","title":"Bug: eval_emit leaks scope on field-default error","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval/emit.rs

Why this is a bug: eval_emit pushes a temporary scope before evaluating event field defaults, but evaluates each default expression with early-return error propagation and only pops the scope after the loop. If any default expression returns a runtime error, the function exits before pop_scope and leaves leaked bindings on the scope stack.

Potential impact: Leaked scope bindings can shadow outer variables and corrupt subsequent evaluation in the same execution context, including error-handling and lifecycle logic that continues using the same Env.

Suggested fix: Wrap the scope-using section in a guard pattern or closure that guarantees pop_scope on all Result paths, matching cleanup patterns used in other eval code.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:08:44Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:08:44Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-mn1u","title":"Bug: List comprehension leaks scope on eval errors","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval/control.rs

Why this is a bug: In eval_list_comprehension, each matched item pushes a scope, but two fallible calls return early before cleanup (eval_expr for filter and eval_expr for element). If either errors, the function exits without env.pop_scope, leaving an extra scope frame.

Potential impact: A runtime error inside a list comprehension can leave interpreter scope state inconsistent, so later evaluation may see incorrect bindings or leaked temporary values.

Suggested fix: Make scope cleanup unconditional for each iteration, for example by storing intermediate Results and popping before propagation, or by using an RAII scope guard that always pops on drop.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:07:42Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:07:42Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ugs1","title":"Bug: Statement soft-keyword dispatch rejects valid identifier statements","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/stmt.rs

Why this is a bug: parse_stmt() unconditionally routes leading identifiers named grant/emit to dedicated statement parsers, and routes revoke unless followed by '('. These are soft keywords (still tokenized as Ident), so valid statements starting with identifiers named grant/emit/revoke (for example assignments or non-call expression statements) are parsed as grant/revoke/emit statements and fail. This contradicts soft-keyword behavior already validated elsewhere (e.g. hook usable as identifier).

Potential impact: Valid programs are rejected with parse diagnostics when users have variables/fields/callees named grant/emit/revoke and use them at statement start, causing incorrect parse failures and skipped statements.

Suggested fix: In parse_stmt(), only dispatch to parse_grant_stmt/parse_revoke_stmt/parse_emit_stmt when lookahead matches each statement form (e.g. grant/revoke followed by an expression that can form entity.Group with required trailing syntax; emit followed by Ident then '('). Otherwise fall back to generic expression/assignment parsing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:28:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:28:58Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ljhg","title":"Bug: list.sum() uses unchecked i64 accumulation","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/methods.rs

Why this is a bug: In eval_list_method, the sum() path adds integers with int_sum += n without checked arithmetic. For large valid int lists this can overflow i64. In debug builds this panics; in release it can wrap to incorrect values, violating the interpreters overflow error semantics used by other integer arithmetic operations.

Potential impact: Calling .sum() on large integer lists can crash the interpreter or return silently incorrect totals.

Suggested fix: Replace unchecked integer addition with checked_add and return RuntimeError when overflow occurs, consistent with other numeric operations.","notes":"Fix both locations: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/methods.rs and crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/collection_builtins.rs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:05:02Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:46:32Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-srhz","title":"Bug: condition id counter not advanced for preassigned IDs","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/reference_state.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: GameState::add_condition preserves non-zero cond.id values but does not advance next_condition_id to stay above preserved IDs. After loading/inserting existing conditions with explicit IDs, later auto-assigned conditions (via apply_condition or add_condition with id=0) can reuse IDs that already exist.\\n\\nPotential impact: Duplicate condition IDs break uniqueness assumptions used for deduplication and identity-based removal, leading to skipped/merged condition processing or removing the wrong condition instance.\\n\\nSuggested fix: When inserting a condition with non-zero id, update next_condition_id = max(next_condition_id, cond.id + 1) using saturating arithmetic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:10:41Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:10:41Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ckbm","title":"Bug: ActiveCondition id overflows when converted to Value::Int","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/state.rs

Why this is a bug: ActiveCondition::to_value stores id as Value::Int(self.id as i64). id is u64, so values above i64::MAX wrap/truncate on cast and become incorrect, even for valid host-provided IDs.

Potential impact: DSL-visible condition IDs can be corrupted, breaking comparisons, lookups, or diagnostics that rely on stable condition instance IDs.

Suggested fix: Preserve full u64 range by exposing id as a lossless representation (for example an unsigned value type or string), or guard and return an error on out-of-range values instead of lossy casting.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:10:29Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:10:29Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-nm22","title":"Bug: Invocation ID allocation panics at u64::MAX","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/lib.rs

Why this is a bug: Interpreter::new_with_invocation_start accepts any u64 start value, including u64::MAX. The next call to alloc_invocation_id performs checked_add(1).expect("invocation ID counter overflow"), which panics instead of returning a runtime error. This is a panic path reachable from valid API inputs.

Potential impact: A host restoring persisted state near or at the numeric limit can crash the process during normal action/reaction execution when an invocation ID is allocated.

Suggested fix: Handle overflow without panicking (for example, make allocation fallible and return RuntimeError, or reject start == u64::MAX in new_with_invocation_start with a clear error).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:09:52Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:09:52Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-a4eg","title":"Bug: remove_condition wraps negative ActiveCondition id to u64","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/builtins.rs

Why this is a bug: In builtin_remove_condition(), the ActiveCondition path reads id as Value::Int and converts it with a direct cast to u64. For negative ids this performs two's-complement wrapping, producing a huge positive u64 instead of rejecting invalid input.

Potential impact: A script can pass an ActiveCondition with id less than 0 and trigger RemoveCondition with an unintended large id, causing silent failure to remove the intended condition or incorrect host-side behavior if ids are interpreted directly.

Suggested fix: Validate id is non-negative before conversion and use checked conversion (for example u64::try_from(id)), returning RuntimeError on invalid values.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:57:48Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:57:48Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-2o22","title":"Bug: if-let/for-range can misparse block start as struct literal","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/expr.rs

Why this is a bug: parse_if_let_expr parses the scrutinee with parse_expr() (not parse_expr_no_struct()), and parse_for_expr parses the range end with parse_expr() before immediately expecting a block. In both places, an identifier followed by {} can be consumed as a struct literal instead of stopping before the enclosing block. This rejects valid forms like if let p = x {} and for i in 0..x {} when the body is empty (or begins with tokens that match struct-literal lookahead).

Potential impact: Valid programs are rejected or misparsed in control-flow constructs, especially for empty bodies and certain body starts.

Suggested fix: Use parse_expr_no_struct() for if let scrutinee and for-range end expressions in parse_for_expr, matching the disambiguation used for if conditions, for sources, and match scrutinees.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:13:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:13:58Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-z7sj","title":"Bug: Reversed compatibility check in list/set methods rejects valid subtype args","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_method.rs

Why this is a bug: types_compatible is defined as types_compatible(actual, expected) and includes one-way compatibility (ActiveCondition is accepted where Condition is expected). In this file, several method checks pass arguments in reverse order (expected first, actual second), including list .append() and set .add()/.remove()/.contains(). This causes false type errors for valid inputs such as passing ActiveCondition where Condition is expected.

Potential impact: Valid programs are incorrectly rejected by the checker when using list/set methods with one-way-compatible element types. This can block compilation for legitimate code paths.

Suggested fix: Swap types_compatible argument order to (actual, expected) in affected checks, e.g. use types_compatible(&elem_ty, &inner) for list append and types_compatible(&elem_ty, inner) for set element methods.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:28:29Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:28:29Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-n4fe","title":"Bug: eval_call dispatch order disagrees with checker for colliding names","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/dispatch.rs

Why this is a bug: eval_call dispatches bare identifier calls in this order: variant then ordinal/from_ordinal/try_from_ordinal then collection builtins then condition then function. The checker (check_call) resolves in a different order: condition then ordinal/collection builtins then variant then function. Condition names are not rejected when they collide with builtins or variants, so a program that type-checks as a condition call can execute a builtin or variant path at runtime instead.

Potential impact: Valid, checked scripts can execute the wrong callable or fail at runtime with type or arity errors when a condition name collides with ordinal/from_ordinal/try_from_ordinal, collection builtins, or a variant name.

Suggested fix: Make runtime dispatch precedence match checker precedence for bare identifier calls (condition first, then special builtins, then variant, then function), or forbid these cross-namespace collisions during collection/checking.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:03:17Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:03:17Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-vkb4","title":"Bug: split_first_token panics on Unicode whitespace","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner/util.rs

Why this is a bug: split_first_token finds the first whitespace byte index with s.find(char::is_whitespace), then slices the remainder as &s[pos + 1..]. For multi-byte Unicode whitespace (for example U+00A0), pos + 1 is not a UTF-8 character boundary, which causes a runtime panic when slicing.

Potential impact: Valid user input containing non-ASCII whitespace can crash CLI command handling instead of returning a parse/usage error.

Suggested fix: Advance by the matched whitespace character length (len_utf8) rather than a hard-coded +1, mirroring the safer implementation used in crates/ttrpg_cli/src/commands.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:53:49Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:53:49Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3k51","title":"Bug: Suppress logic skips AnyEntity event values","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/event.rs

Why this is a bug: is_suppressed only collects event payload values when the declared type matches Ty::Entity(_). The checker resolves the DSL entity type to Ty::AnyEntity, and suppress clauses are validated against those params/fields. For events using entity-typed params or computed fields, suppression never evaluates conditions on those entities, so matching suppress clauses are silently ignored.

Potential impact: Reactions that should be blocked by condition suppress clauses can still trigger for events that use generic entity params/fields, causing incorrect rules behavior.

Suggested fix: Treat both Ty::Entity(_) and Ty::AnyEntity as entity-bearing in is_suppressed when collecting event params/fields, and require payload values to be Value::Entity in both cases.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:53:33Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:53:33Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-7bdk","title":"Bug: Condition/event default params cannot reference earlier params","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check.rs

Why this is a bug: check_condition and check_event type-check parameter defaults inside a fresh scope but never bind previously declared parameters before checking later defaults. This differs from bind_params (used for functions/actions/prompts), which binds params incrementally so defaults like b: int = a are valid. As written, valid condition/event defaults that reference earlier params are incorrectly reported as undefined/type errors.

Potential impact: Valid DSL programs are rejected during checking when condition/event defaults depend on prior parameters, despite runtime argument binding supporting this pattern.

Suggested fix: Reuse bind_params-style incremental binding for condition/event default validation (or explicitly bind prior params in order while checking defaults), so earlier parameters are in scope for later default expressions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:27:03Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:27:03Z"}

new_value: Duplicate of tdsl-ebm8. Same spawn duplicate-group issue at two layers; fix at parse layer makes mutation layer moot.

{"id":"tdsl-fdy0","title":"Bug: parse_type rejects qualified types with option alias","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/types.rs

Why this is a bug: In parse_type, the "option" branch only handles option<T> and otherwise returns TypeExpr::Named("option") immediately. Unlike the generic fallback path, it never checks for .Ident to form TypeExpr::Qualified. This means valid qualified types such as option.CustomType (when use "Other" as option is present) are misparsed, leaving .CustomType unconsumed and causing downstream parse errors.

Potential impact: Programs that use option as a module alias cannot reference imported types via qualified syntax, producing parse failures in otherwise valid code.

Suggested fix: In the "option" case, keep option<T> handling, but for non-< cases fall through to the same named/qualified logic used by the default identifier branch (including .Ident handling) instead of returning Named("option") directly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:57:49Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:57:49Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-8t11","title":"Bug: Pattern parser rejects qualified names starting with 'some'","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/pattern.rs

Why this is a bug: In parse_pattern, the raw_name == 'some' recovery branch runs before qualified-pattern handling. Inputs like some.Variant or some.Variant(x) are valid qualified patterns when a type or alias is named some, but they are misparsed as bare some and converted to PatternKind::Some(_) with a diagnostic.

Potential impact: Valid match, if-let, and for patterns become unparsable or are parsed incorrectly when the leading type or alias identifier is some, causing false errors and broken behavior in affected code.

Suggested fix: Only apply bare-some recovery when the next token is not Dot; allow the existing qualified-pattern path (IDENT.DOT.IDENT...) to run first for some.* forms.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:55:49Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:55:49Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-qfdw","title":"Bug: Recovery skips group declarations","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/parser.rs

Why this is a bug: recover_to_decl() uses is_decl_start() to stop at the next declaration after an error, but is_decl_start() does not include the group keyword while parse_decl() does parse group declarations. During recovery, valid group declarations can be skipped instead of being parsed.

Potential impact: After a parse error inside a system block, subsequent valid group declarations may be dropped, causing cascading diagnostics and missing AST nodes for later compilation stages.

Suggested fix: Add "group" to is_decl_start() so recovery and declaration parsing recognize the same declaration starters.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:35:41Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:35:41Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-tt3v","title":"Bug: load glob errors keep stale program and reload target","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner/load.rs

Why this is a bug: cmd_load returns early on glob expansion errors (invalid pattern or no matches) before calling load_paths. Those early returns skip the stale-state reset performed in load_paths error paths. As a result, a failed load due to glob resolution leaves the previous program, handles, and last_paths intact.

Potential impact: After a failed load with a bad glob, commands can continue operating on the old program, and reload loads old files instead of the failed target set. This is inconsistent with other load failure modes and can cause users to execute against unintended game rules.

Suggested fix: Route glob-resolution failures through the same failure-state handling as other load errors, or perform equivalent state reset plus last_paths update in cmd_load before returning.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:52:03Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:52:03Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ihp4","title":"Bug: inspect misreports unknown Group.field as not granted","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner/inspect.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: In cmd_inspect, the three-segment path branch (handle.Group.field) reads state first and, when the Group key is absent, falls back to reporting '\u003chandle\u003e.\u003cGroup\u003e is not currently granted' unless the group is declared as required. For an undeclared group name (e.g. inspect hero.Foo.bar where Foo is not a group/field on the entity type), this produces an incorrect diagnostic instead of an unknown group/field error.\\n\\nPotential impact: Users receive misleading errors for typos and invalid inspect paths, which obscures actual schema problems and makes debugging harder.\\n\\nSuggested fix: In the handle.Group.field branch, validate group_name against the entity type schema first (lookup_optional_group) and return an unknown-group error when absent; only use granted/missing status messaging for declared groups.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:51:34Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:51:34Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-facb","title":"Bug: Completion span misaligned after trailing space","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/completer.rs

Why this is a bug: In the do, call, enable, and disable branches, the current token is derived with rest.trim(), but replacement start is computed as pos - current.len(). When the cursor is after a trailing space (for example do Attack ), trim() drops that space and word_start shifts right by one (or more), so the replacement span no longer matches the actual token. Accepting a completion can replace the wrong substring and corrupt input (for example producing do AAttack).

Potential impact: Tab completion can modify valid user input into invalid commands, causing confusing behavior and failed command execution in common interactive workflows.

Suggested fix: Derive the active token without trimming trailing whitespace (for example use a last-token parser like last_word(rest)), or detect trailing whitespace and avoid suggesting/replacing the previous token; compute span start from the true token boundary in line_to_cursor.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:36:36Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:36:36Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3mo1","title":"Bug: load command misparses paths beginning with //","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/commands.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: In parse_command(), the load branch stops parsing when a token starts with //, assuming it is always a comment marker. Valid paths can begin with // (for example UNC-style paths like //server/share/file), so these inputs are incorrectly parsed as no path and become Unknown.\\n\\nPotential impact: Legitimate load commands can fail unexpectedly for users with paths that start with //, preventing files from being loaded.\\n\\nSuggested fix: In the load branch, only treat // as a comment delimiter when it appears after at least one path token, or use quote/comment-aware parsing that does not classify a leading // path token as a comment.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:34:54Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:34:54Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-olhd","title":"Bug: Multiline spans render incorrect caret underline","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_ast/src/diagnostic.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: render_diagnostic computes caret_len as span.end - span.start and always prints the carets on a single source line. For spans that cross a newline, this includes bytes from subsequent lines and newline characters, so the underline is rendered on the first line with an incorrect (often very long) width.\\n\\nPotential impact: Diagnostics for valid multiline spans point to the wrong region and can become misleading or noisy, reducing debuggability.\\n\\nSuggested fix: When rendering single-line output, cap the underline length to the remaining bytes in the start line (line_text_end - local_start). Optionally add dedicated multiline rendering for cross-line spans.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:23:26Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:23:26Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-733o","title":"Bug: Span::merge allows cross-file merge in release builds","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_ast/src/span.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: Span::merge relies on debug_assert_eq!(self.file.0, other.file.0), which is compiled out in release builds. When called with spans from different files, it silently returns a merged span that keeps self.file but combines start/end from both spans, producing an invalid source location.\\n\\nPotential impact: Incorrect source mapping in diagnostics or downstream logic, with hard-to-trace misattribution of errors to the wrong file.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Enforce same-file merging in all builds (e.g., assert!/panic, Result-returning API, or explicit cross-file handling) instead of debug_assert-only validation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:22:56Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:22:56Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-zug6","title":"Bug: parse_multi FileId truncation and SYNTH collision","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lib.rs

Why this is a bug: parse_multi assigns file ids with FileId(i as u32). For i == u32::MAX, this produces the reserved sentinel FileId::SYNTH, and for larger i values it silently wraps/truncates. This creates non-unique or synthetic file ids for real files.

Potential impact: Diagnostics and span ownership can be attributed to the wrong file or treated as synthetic/no-source locations when loading very large source sets. This breaks correctness and can hide source locations.

Suggested fix: Use u32::try_from(i) and emit or propagate a hard diagnostic when conversion fails, and explicitly reject u32::MAX so real files never receive FileId::SYNTH.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:12:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:12:58Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3kyz","title":"Bug: Move names can bypass reserved __ prefix validation","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lower.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: User-authored move names are copied directly into a synthesized ActionDecl with synthetic=true, and moves are removed before checker collection. Reserved-name validation in checker only runs for non-synthetic actions, so a move named with a reserved '__' prefix (for example, '__hack') is accepted even though user declarations are supposed to reject reserved internal prefixes.\\n\\nPotential impact: Users can define externally callable declarations in the reserved internal namespace, undermining the reserved-prefix safety rule and increasing risk of collisions/confusing behavior with internal generated names.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Validate move names against the reserved '__' prefix during lowering (or preserve user-origin metadata so checker still enforces reserved-name rules on lowered user declarations).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:14:14Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:14:14Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-4567","title":"Bug: Multiline struct literals are not detected by lookahead","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/expr.rs

Why this is a bug: is_struct_lit_start() only recognizes IDENT { } and IDENT { IDENT : ... } using fixed token offsets. It does not skip Newline tokens after the opening brace. As a result, expressions like Point {\n  x: 1\n} or Point {\n} are not recognized as struct literals even though parse_struct_lit() explicitly supports newlines via skip_newlines().

Potential impact: Valid multiline struct literals are rejected/misparsed, forcing single-line formatting and causing confusing errors in normal code formatting.

Suggested fix: Make struct-literal lookahead newline-tolerant (skip Newline tokens while probing tokens after LBrace), or parse with a tentative/backtracking strategy instead of fixed offsets.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:14:02Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:14:02Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-2o22","title":"Bug: if-let/for-range can misparse block start as struct literal","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/expr.rs

Why this is a bug: parse_if_let_expr parses the scrutinee with parse_expr() (not parse_expr_no_struct()), and parse_for_expr parses the range end with parse_expr() before immediately expecting a block. In both places, an identifier followed by {} can be consumed as a struct literal instead of stopping before the enclosing block. This rejects valid forms like if let p = x {} and for i in 0..x {} when the body is empty (or begins with tokens that match struct-literal lookahead).

Potential impact: Valid programs are rejected or misparsed in control-flow constructs, especially for empty bodies and certain body starts.

Suggested fix: Use parse_expr_no_struct() for if let scrutinee and for-range end expressions in parse_for_expr, matching the disambiguation used for if conditions, for sources, and match scrutinees.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:13:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:39Z"}

Potential impact: Valid DSL programs are rejected during checking when condition/event defaults depend on prior parameters, despite runtime argument binding supporting this pattern.

Suggested fix: Reuse bind_params-style incremental binding for condition/event default validation (or explicitly bind prior params in order while checking defaults), so earlier parameters are in scope for later default expressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:27:03Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:40Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-5zuy","title":"Bug: if/if let branches ignore expected type hints","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_control.rs

Why this is a bug: check_expr_expecting carries expected-type hints to disambiguate variants and infer literals, but ExprKind::If and ExprKind::IfLet call into check_if/check_if_let which currently type-check branches via check_block/check_expr without threading any hint. This drops contextual type information for branch bodies. As a result, valid code can fail with ambiguity/type errors (for example, bare variants that require an expected enum hint, or branch literals that rely on expected element types).

Potential impact: Valid if/if let expressions in typed contexts (return positions, argument positions, etc.) can be rejected with false type errors, while equivalent match expressions type-check correctly because they do thread hints. This is a user-visible type-checking inconsistency.

Suggested fix: Add an optional expected-type hint parameter to check_if/check_if_let and check_else_branch_type, then check then/else blocks with check_block_with_tail_hint(..., hint) and nested else-if expressions with check_expr_expecting(..., hint) so branch inference/disambiguation uses context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:26:52Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:26:52Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-j5c2","title":"Bug: Qualified types in modify selectors are not resolved","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/resolve.rs

Why this is a bug: desugar_qualified_types claims to walk all type expressions, but desugar_decl_types only calls desugar_modify_stmts for modify clauses and never visits ModifyClause.target. Selector predicates in ModifyTarget::Selector can contain TypeExpr via SelectorPredicate::Returns and SelectorPredicate::HasParam { ty }. Qualified types in those positions remain as TypeExpr::Qualified, so checker code later reports qualified type requires module resolution instead of resolving valid aliases.

Potential impact: Valid programs using qualified types in modify selectors fail type checking with false errors; alias and type ownership validation is skipped for these type positions.

Suggested fix: Extend resolver traversal to desugar selector predicate types in ModifyClause.target (for both condition and option modify clauses), recursing through Returns and optional HasParam type expressions before checking.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:35:19Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:35:19Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-4t1y","title":"Bug: Inherited modify bindings use child receiver name","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/pipeline.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: collect_modifiers_owned iterates modify clauses from ancestor conditions, but check_modify_bindings always looks up the active (child) condition declaration and binds only that receiver name when evaluating clause bindings. If a parent condition uses a different receiver identifier than the child, binding expressions in inherited parent clauses are evaluated with the wrong scope and can fail or mismatch even though the script is valid.\\n\\nPotential impact: Valid inherited condition modifiers can be skipped or raise runtime errors when receiver names differ across parent/child conditions, causing incorrect modify behavior in production execution.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Pass the clause’s declaration receiver name (the current cond_decl.receiver_name from collect_modifiers_owned) into check_modify_bindings and bind that name while evaluating bindings for that clause.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:10:01Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:10:01Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-t0sn","title":"Bug: Cost modify bindings compare against actor for all names","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/action.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: In collect_and_apply_cost_modifiers(), binding matching sets param_val to Value::Entity(*actor) for every binding, regardless of binding.name. For modify Action.cost clauses that bind an action parameter (not the receiver), runtime compares the evaluated binding expression against the actor entity instead of the actual argument value, so explicit parameter bindings cannot match correctly.\\n\\nPotential impact: Condition-based cost modifiers with parameter constraints are skipped or evaluated incorrectly, causing wrong cost deduction behavior in valid programs.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Resolve each binding.name to the target call value (receiver -\u003e actor, param names -\u003e currently bound argument values) before comparison, mirroring the generic modify binding logic used in pipeline.rs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:56:22Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:56:22Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-figw","title":"Bug: Action method defaults cannot reference receiver","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/actions.rs

Why this is a bug: In dispatch_action_method, bind_args is called with only fn_info.params and action_decl.params, excluding the receiver from the binding context. bind_args evaluates default expressions in a temporary scope containing already-bound parameters. For action defaults that reference the receiver (allowed by checker because receiver is in scope when defaults are type-checked), method-call syntax (entity.Action()) evaluates those defaults without receiver bound and raises an undefined-variable runtime error on valid programs.

Potential impact: Valid DSL programs fail at runtime when calling actions with omitted optional args whose defaults reference the receiver, but the equivalent function-style action call works. This is inconsistent behavior between call syntaxes and causes unexpected runtime failures.

Suggested fix: In dispatch_action_method, evaluate defaults in an effective parameter context that includes the receiver as a first bound slot (mirroring dispatch_action), or explicitly push a scope and bind the receiver name/value before calling bind_args so default expressions can resolve it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:59:09Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:59:09Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3k51","title":"Bug: Suppress logic skips AnyEntity event values","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/event.rs

Why this is a bug: is_suppressed only collects event payload values when the declared type matches Ty::Entity(_). The checker resolves the DSL entity type to Ty::AnyEntity, and suppress clauses are validated against those params/fields. For events using entity-typed params or computed fields, suppression never evaluates conditions on those entities, so matching suppress clauses are silently ignored.

Potential impact: Reactions that should be blocked by condition suppress clauses can still trigger for events that use generic entity params/fields, causing incorrect rules behavior.

Suggested fix: Treat both Ty::Entity(_) and Ty::AnyEntity as entity-bearing in is_suppressed when collecting event params/fields, and require payload values to be Value::Entity in both cases.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:53:33Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:40Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-vkb4","title":"Bug: split_first_token panics on Unicode whitespace","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner/util.rs

Why this is a bug: split_first_token finds the first whitespace byte index with s.find(char::is_whitespace), then slices the remainder as &s[pos + 1..]. For multi-byte Unicode whitespace (for example U+00A0), pos + 1 is not a UTF-8 character boundary, which causes a runtime panic when slicing.

Potential impact: Valid user input containing non-ASCII whitespace can crash CLI command handling instead of returning a parse/usage error.

Suggested fix: Advance by the matched whitespace character length (len_utf8) rather than a hard-coded +1, mirroring the safer implementation used in crates/ttrpg_cli/src/commands.rs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:53:49Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:40Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-8qat","title":"Bug: Option extends cycle error builder can loop forever","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/collect.rs

Why this is a bug: In validate_option_extends(), cycle detection notices revisits via visited, but the error-chain construction assumes the cycle closes back to start_name. For a graph like A->B->C->B, starting from A detects a cycle at B; the chain builder then walks A->B->C->B->... and never reaches A, so the while-loop never terminates.

Potential impact: The checker can hang indefinitely on invalid option-extends input, blocking diagnostics and potentially wedging CLI/editor workflows.

Suggested fix: When building the cycle message, stop when revisiting any node (or when revisiting the detected cycle node) instead of requiring parent == start_name; alternatively track predecessor/path during detection and reconstruct only the actual cycle segment.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:31:32Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:31:32Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-bbbr","title":"Bug: Empty trigger binding list rejected","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/decl.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: parse_trigger_param() always calls parse_trigger_bindings(), and parse_trigger_bindings() unconditionally parses at least one binding. This makes trigger clauses with empty binding lists unparseable. However, the AST/checker/runtime all support empty trigger binding vectors (matching by event name only), so parser behavior is inconsistent with the rest of the pipeline.\\n\\nPotential impact: Reactions/hooks cannot target events without explicit bindings (including zero-parameter events), causing valid programs to fail at parse time.\\n\\nSuggested fix: In parse_trigger_param(), mirror parse_modify_clause behavior: if the next token is RParen, use an empty vec; otherwise call parse_trigger_bindings().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:36:47Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:36:47Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-icp7","title":"Bug: Empty suppress binding list rejected","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/decl.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: parse_suppress_clause() always calls parse_modify_bindings(), and parse_modify_bindings() unconditionally parses at least one binding. This makes suppress clauses with empty binding lists unparseable. Downstream code handles empty suppress bindings (interpreted as unconditional match for the event), so parser behavior is inconsistent with checker/runtime semantics.\\n\\nPotential impact: Conditions cannot express unconditional suppression of an event, and otherwise valid suppress clauses fail during parsing.\\n\\nSuggested fix: In parse_suppress_clause(), allow empty parentheses by checking for RParen before calling parse_modify_bindings().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:36:47Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:36:47Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3947","title":"Bug: eval_emit leaks scope on field-default error","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval/emit.rs

Why this is a bug: eval_emit pushes a temporary scope before evaluating event field defaults, but evaluates each default expression with early-return error propagation and only pops the scope after the loop. If any default expression returns a runtime error, the function exits before pop_scope and leaves leaked bindings on the scope stack.

Potential impact: Leaked scope bindings can shadow outer variables and corrupt subsequent evaluation in the same execution context, including error-handling and lifecycle logic that continues using the same Env.

Suggested fix: Wrap the scope-using section in a guard pattern or closure that guarantees pop_scope on all Result paths, matching cleanup patterns used in other eval code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:08:44Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:46:36Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-mn1u","title":"Bug: List comprehension leaks scope on eval errors","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval/control.rs

Why this is a bug: In eval_list_comprehension, each matched item pushes a scope, but two fallible calls return early before cleanup (eval_expr for filter and eval_expr for element). If either errors, the function exits without env.pop_scope, leaving an extra scope frame.

Potential impact: A runtime error inside a list comprehension can leave interpreter scope state inconsistent, so later evaluation may see incorrect bindings or leaked temporary values.

Suggested fix: Make scope cleanup unconditional for each iteration, for example by storing intermediate Results and popping before propagation, or by using an RAII scope guard that always pops on drop.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:07:42Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:46:37Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-qfd0","title":"Bug: Collection builtins accept and ignore extra arguments","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/collection_builtins.rs

Why this is a bug: Most builtins in this file only check for too-few args (for example args.is_empty() or args.len() < 2) and then evaluate only the first required arguments, silently ignoring extras. This contradicts declared builtin arities and checker behavior (which expects exact counts), and it means calls like len(x, y) can succeed instead of erroring.

Potential impact: In unchecked expression-evaluation paths, invalid calls are accepted, extra argument expressions are skipped, and expected side effects or errors from those expressions do not occur.

Suggested fix: Enforce exact arity in each builtin (args.len() == required count) and return an arity RuntimeError when the count does not match.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:01:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:01:58Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-2ysd","title":"Bug: Table value-dispatch ignores argument arity","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/functions.rs

Why this is a bug: dispatch_table_with_values forwards raw Vec<Value> to dispatch_table_core without validating arity, and dispatch_table_core compares keys with arguments using entry.keys.iter().zip(arg_values.iter()). zip truncates to the shorter length, so calls with too few or too many arguments can still match entries instead of erroring. Example: calling a one argument table with zero arguments makes the key loop skip entirely and the first entry can match.

Potential impact: Public API calls through Interpreter.evaluate_derive when targeting a table can return incorrect table results silently on wrong arity inputs, causing incorrect game logic instead of a runtime error.

Suggested fix: Validate arg_values.len() against table parameter count before matching in dispatch_table_with_values or dispatch_table_core, and return a clear arity error on mismatch. Optionally also guard inside core to prevent future callers from bypassing the check.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:03:26Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:03:26Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ljhg","title":"Bug: list.sum() uses unchecked i64 accumulation","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/methods.rs

Why this is a bug: In eval_list_method, the sum() path adds integers with int_sum += n without checked arithmetic. For large valid int lists this can overflow i64. In debug builds this panics; in release it can wrap to incorrect values, violating the interpreters overflow error semantics used by other integer arithmetic operations.

Potential impact: Calling .sum() on large integer lists can crash the interpreter or return silently incorrect totals.

Suggested fix: Replace unchecked integer addition with checked_add and return RuntimeError when overflow occurs, consistent with other numeric operations.","notes":"Fix both locations: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/methods.rs and crates/ttrpg_interp/src/call/collection_builtins.rs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:05:02Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:46:37Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-6ajy","title":"Bug: Method-style action calls bypass visibility checks","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_call.rs

Why this is a bug: In check_call, the ExprKind::FieldAccess path treats entity-dot-action calls as action calls by looking up the field in env.lookup_fn and returning early, but it never calls check_name_visible for the action name. Direct calls do enforce visibility via check_name_visible with Namespace::Function. This creates inconsistent semantics and allows calling non-visible actions via method syntax.

Potential impact: Code that should fail module visibility/import checks can type-check successfully when rewritten as entity.action(...), bypassing encapsulation boundaries and incorrectly accepting invalid programs.

Suggested fix: In the method-style action branch, run the same non-builtin visibility check as direct calls (for example, check_name_visible(field, Namespace::Function, span)) before validating arguments and return type.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:50:06Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:50:06Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-0rrc","title":"Bug: emit bypasses event visibility checks","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_checker/src/check_stmt.rs

Why this is a bug: check_emit looks up events in self.env.events but never calls check_name_visible with Namespace::Event. In multi-system mode, this allows emitting events defined in other systems without a required use, unlike other event references such as triggers and suppress clauses which enforce visibility.

Potential impact: Programs can compile while violating declared module/system boundaries, causing inconsistent name-resolution behavior and bypassing import rules.

Suggested fix: In check_emit, call self.check_name_visible(event_name, Namespace::Event, span) before or alongside the event lookup, matching visibility checks in other event-reference paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:31:13Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:31:13Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-fdy0","title":"Bug: parse_type rejects qualified types with option alias","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/types.rs

Why this is a bug: In parse_type, the "option" branch only handles option<T> and otherwise returns TypeExpr::Named("option") immediately. Unlike the generic fallback path, it never checks for .Ident to form TypeExpr::Qualified. This means valid qualified types such as option.CustomType (when use "Other" as option is present) are misparsed, leaving .CustomType unconsumed and causing downstream parse errors.

Potential impact: Programs that use option as a module alias cannot reference imported types via qualified syntax, producing parse failures in otherwise valid code.

Suggested fix: In the "option" case, keep option<T> handling, but for non-< cases fall through to the same named/qualified logic used by the default identifier branch (including .Ident handling) instead of returning Named("option") directly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:57:49Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:45Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-8t11","title":"Bug: Pattern parser rejects qualified names starting with 'some'","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/pattern.rs

Why this is a bug: In parse_pattern, the raw_name == 'some' recovery branch runs before qualified-pattern handling. Inputs like some.Variant or some.Variant(x) are valid qualified patterns when a type or alias is named some, but they are misparsed as bare some and converted to PatternKind::Some(_) with a diagnostic.

Potential impact: Valid match, if-let, and for patterns become unparsable or are parsed incorrectly when the leading type or alias identifier is some, causing false errors and broken behavior in affected code.

Suggested fix: Only apply bare-some recovery when the next token is not Dot; allow the existing qualified-pattern path (IDENT.DOT.IDENT...) to run first for some.* forms.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:55:49Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ugs1","title":"Bug: Statement soft-keyword dispatch rejects valid identifier statements","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/stmt.rs

Why this is a bug: parse_stmt() unconditionally routes leading identifiers named grant/emit to dedicated statement parsers, and routes revoke unless followed by '('. These are soft keywords (still tokenized as Ident), so valid statements starting with identifiers named grant/emit/revoke (for example assignments or non-call expression statements) are parsed as grant/revoke/emit statements and fail. This contradicts soft-keyword behavior already validated elsewhere (e.g. hook usable as identifier).

Potential impact: Valid programs are rejected with parse diagnostics when users have variables/fields/callees named grant/emit/revoke and use them at statement start, causing incorrect parse failures and skipped statements.

Suggested fix: In parse_stmt(), only dispatch to parse_grant_stmt/parse_revoke_stmt/parse_emit_stmt when lookahead matches each statement form (e.g. grant/revoke followed by an expression that can form entity.Group with required trailing syntax; emit followed by Ident then '('). Otherwise fall back to generic expression/assignment parsing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:28:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:46:37Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-j5dx","title":"Load glob errors keep stale state","description":"cmd_load returns early on glob errors without calling clear_state(), leaving stale program/handles/last_paths from a previous load","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T05:52:44Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T05:52:44Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-6jwr","title":"Load misparses paths beginning with //","description":"The load branch naively breaks on any token starting with //, but // can appear in valid file paths","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T05:52:46Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T05:52:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-bmaw","title":"Inspect misreports unknown Group.field as not granted","description":"When gs.read_field returns None in the three-segment branch, lookup_optional_group returning None (undeclared) is treated same as declared-but-not-granted","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T05:52:48Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T05:52:48Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-7x3t","title":"Bug: Queue dice validation diverges from normal d0 handling","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/effects.rs

Why this is a bug: roll_dice normalizes sides with (group.sides as i64).max(1), so d0 is treated as 1..=1. roll_dice_from_queue validates queued values against 1..=group.sides directly, so with sides == 0 it rejects all queued values and vetoes.

Potential impact: Queued deterministic rolls can fail while non-queued rolls succeed for the same expression, causing inconsistent runtime behavior.

Suggested fix: Normalize sides in roll_dice_from_queue the same way as roll_dice before validation and fallback RNG.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:57:15Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:57:15Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-kymh","title":"Bug: Nested struct resource bounds use wrong lookup prefix","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/eval/helpers.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: resolve_resource_bounds only passes a one-segment prefix for group fields (or no prefix for normal fields) into eval_bound_expr. When a resource type is nested in a named struct (for example stats.hp: resource(0..=max_hp)), identifier lookup in the bound expression resolves max_hp as a top-level entity field instead of stats.max_hp (or Group.stats.max_hp). That causes bound evaluation to return None and disables clamping for valid nested resource declarations.\\n\\nPotential impact: Assignments to nested resource fields can bypass min/max enforcement and write invalid values (for example negative HP) whenever bounds reference sibling fields in the same nested struct.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Track and pass the full container path prefix to eval_bound_expr (all consumed FieldPathSegment::Field segments leading to the resource container), not just the optional-group root segment. Use that full prefix when resolving identifier names from resource bound expressions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:53:19Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:53:19Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-crkv","title":"Bug: Cost modify binding eval errors are silently ignored","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_interp/src/action.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: In collect_and_apply_cost_modifiers(), binding expressions are evaluated with eval_expr, but Err(_) is converted into all_match = false rather than being returned. This suppresses runtime expression errors during cost-modifier binding checks.\\n\\nPotential impact: Valid programs can silently skip applicable cost modifiers when a binding expression hits a runtime error (for example division by zero), producing incorrect behavior and making failures hard to diagnose.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Propagate eval_expr errors from binding evaluation (return Err) instead of treating them as a non-match, aligning behavior with modify binding checks in pipeline.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:56:38Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:56:38Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-qfdw","title":"Bug: Recovery skips group declarations","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/parser.rs

Why this is a bug: recover_to_decl() uses is_decl_start() to stop at the next declaration after an error, but is_decl_start() does not include the group keyword while parse_decl() does parse group declarations. During recovery, valid group declarations can be skipped instead of being parsed.

Potential impact: After a parse error inside a system block, subsequent valid group declarations may be dropped, causing cascading diagnostics and missing AST nodes for later compilation stages.

Suggested fix: Add "group" to is_decl_start() so recovery and declaration parsing recognize the same declaration starters.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:35:41Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-3kyz","title":"Bug: Move names can bypass reserved __ prefix validation","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lower.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: User-authored move names are copied directly into a synthesized ActionDecl with synthetic=true, and moves are removed before checker collection. Reserved-name validation in checker only runs for non-synthetic actions, so a move named with a reserved '__' prefix (for example, '__hack') is accepted even though user declarations are supposed to reject reserved internal prefixes.\\n\\nPotential impact: Users can define externally callable declarations in the reserved internal namespace, undermining the reserved-prefix safety rule and increasing risk of collisions/confusing behavior with internal generated names.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Validate move names against the reserved '__' prefix during lowering (or preserve user-origin metadata so checker still enforces reserved-name rules on lowered user declarations).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:14:14Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-zug6","title":"Bug: parse_multi FileId truncation and SYNTH collision","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_parser/src/lib.rs

Why this is a bug: parse_multi assigns file ids with FileId(i as u32). For i == u32::MAX, this produces the reserved sentinel FileId::SYNTH, and for larger i values it silently wraps/truncates. This creates non-unique or synthetic file ids for real files.

Potential impact: Diagnostics and span ownership can be attributed to the wrong file or treated as synthetic/no-source locations when loading very large source sets. This breaks correctness and can hide source locations.

Suggested fix: Use u32::try_from(i) and emit or propagate a hard diagnostic when conversion fails, and explicitly reject u32::MAX so real files never receive FileId::SYNTH.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:12:58Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-ebm8","title":"Bug: spawn allows duplicate inline groups with silent overwrite","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_cli/src/runner/parse.rs

Why this is a bug: parse_spawn_block rejects duplicate field keys but does not reject duplicate inline group names. The returned groups vector can contain the same group multiple times, and cmd_spawn later writes each prepared group in order. This means a single spawn command can declare the same group twice and the last one silently wins, which is inconsistent with duplicate field handling and makes input order change semantics unexpectedly.

Potential impact: Users can accidentally provide conflicting values for the same optional group in one spawn block and get silently overwritten state instead of a validation error, leading to hard-to-debug incorrect character/entity setup.

Suggested fix: Track seen group names in parse_spawn_block (or before applying prepared groups) and return an explicit duplicate-group error when a name repeats, matching duplicate field behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:52:34Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-01T23:52:34Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-olhd","title":"Bug: Multiline spans render incorrect caret underline","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_ast/src/diagnostic.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: render_diagnostic computes caret_len as span.end - span.start and always prints the carets on a single source line. For spans that cross a newline, this includes bytes from subsequent lines and newline characters, so the underline is rendered on the first line with an incorrect (often very long) width.\\n\\nPotential impact: Diagnostics for valid multiline spans point to the wrong region and can become misleading or noisy, reducing debuggability.\\n\\nSuggested fix: When rendering single-line output, cap the underline length to the remaining bytes in the start line (line_text_end - local_start). Optionally add dedicated multiline rendering for cross-line spans.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:23:26Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-733o","title":"Bug: Span::merge allows cross-file merge in release builds","description":"File: crates/ttrpg_ast/src/span.rs\\n\\nWhy this is a bug: Span::merge relies on debug_assert_eq!(self.file.0, other.file.0), which is compiled out in release builds. When called with spans from different files, it silently returns a merged span that keeps self.file but combines start/end from both spans, producing an invalid source location.\\n\\nPotential impact: Incorrect source mapping in diagnostics or downstream logic, with hard-to-trace misattribution of errors to the wrong file.\\n\\nSuggested fix: Enforce same-file merging in all builds (e.g., assert!/panic, Result-returning API, or explicit cross-file handling) instead of debug_assert-only validation.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T23:22:56Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:06:46Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-czkm","title":"Fix test isolation for parallel test runs","description":"Runner unit tests in ttrpg_cli/src/runner/tests.rs write to fixed paths (temp_dir/ttrpg_cli_test/test_load.ttrpg). Parallel cargo test can race. The invocation tests already solve this with AtomicU64 counters. Apply the same pattern or use the tempfile crate for proper isolation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:19:22Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T06:19:22Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-8bb1","title":"OSRIC: Ability score modifier tables","description":"Create osric/osric_ability.ttrpg with table declarations for all 6 ability score modifier lookups from OSRIC 3.0 Chapter One. old_value: {"id":"tdsl-cid0","title":"OSRIC: Core four classes (Fighter, Cleric, Magic-User, Thief)","description":"Create osric/osric_classes.ttrpg with the 4 core classes as optional groups. Each class needs: minimum ability score requirements, hit die type, BTHB (base to-hit bonus) per level from p.81 ascending AC table, saving throw targets per level (5 categories), weapon proficiency count and non-proficiency penalty, allowed armour/weapons. Use table declarations for per-class per-level progressions. Fighter: d10 HD, any armour/weapons, best BTHB, heroic assault at 2, melee attack combo at 7/13, mighty blow at 8. Cleric: d8 HD, blunt weapons only, spell slots per level, turn undead. Magic-User: d4 HD, no armour, dagger/dart/oil/staff only, spell slots + spell book. Thief: d6 HD, leather armour, percentage-based skills (climb/hide/listen/pick locks/pick pockets/read languages/move quietly/traps) with per-level progression tables.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:42:38Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:42:38Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-slbl","title":"OSRIC: Saving throw system","description":"Create saving throw mechanics in osric/osric_combat.ttrpg or osric_saves.ttrpg. OSRIC has 5 saving throw categories: Aimed Magic Items, Breath Weapons, Death/Paralysis/Poison, Petrification/Polymorph, Spells. Each class has a unique table of target numbers per level range. Use table declarations: table save_target(class: CharacterClass, level: int, category: SaveCategory) -\u003e int. Include mechanic saving_throw(char, category) -\u003e bool that rolls d20 \u003e= target. Apply modifiers: WIS mental save mod for charm/mind spells, DEX agility save mod for aimed magic/area effects, racial bonuses (dwarves +4 vs magic, halflings +4, etc). Model after ose/ose_saves.ttrpg patterns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:04Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:54:08Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T23:54:08Z","close_reason":"Saving throw tables will be added to osric_saves.ttrpg"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-4n35","title":"OSRIC: Equipment — weapons and armour","description":"Create osric/osric_equipment.ttrpg with weapon and armour definitions from OSRIC 3.0 Chapter Four. Weapons need: name, damage vs S-M, damage vs L, length, rate of fire, range increments (-2 per increment), speed factor, space required, encumbrance weight. Key weapons: longsword (1d8/1d12), short sword (1d6/1d8), dagger (1d4/1d3, SF2), mace (1d6+1/1d6, blunt), longbow (1d6/1d6, ROF 2), light crossbow (1d4+1/1d4+1, ROF 1). Armour: type, weight, movement cap, AC (ascending), cost. Key armour: leather (AC 12, 120ft), chain mail (AC 15, 90ft), plate mail (AC 17, 60ft), shields (small/medium/large, +1 AC vs 1/2/3 attacks per round). Include weapon speed factor as int field for initiative tie-breaking.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:29Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:29Z"}

{"id":"tdsl-xliw","title":"OSRIC: Basic conditions","description":"Create osric/osric_conditions.ttrpg with the core combat conditions from OSRIC 3.0 p.96-102. Conditions to model: Invisible (attacked at -4 if general location known, +4 on all saves, can't be attacked if location unknown), Prone (attacked at +4, can't use shield/DEX AC bonus), Stunned (attacked at +4, can't use shield/DEX AC bonus), Paralyzed (auto-hit, max damage at melee range, instant kill/bind if unprotected), Sleeping (depends on magical vs natural sleep), Staggered (attacked at +2). Use condition blocks with modify clauses to alter attack rolls and AC computations. Include concealment and cover modifiers (1/4 to total concealment: -1 to -4 penalty on attacks; 25% to 90% cover: -2 to -10).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:22Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:22Z"}

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-k4su","title":"OSRIC: Combat system (ascending AC, attack rolls, damage)","description":"Create osric/osric_combat.ttrpg with the core combat resolution mechanics using ascending AC (p.80-81). Mechanics needed: melee_attack (d20 + BTHB + STR mod + weapon mods \u003e= target AC, natural 1 auto-miss, natural 20 gets +5 bonus), missile_attack (d20 + BTHB + DEX missile mod \u003e= AC), damage_roll (weapon dice + STR damage mod for melee/thrown). Include combat actions as action blocks: MeleeAttack, MissileAttack, Charge (+2 to hit, double move, defender with longer weapon strikes first, set weapon does double damage), Parry (subtract STR/specialisation to-hit from enemy attacks). Events: Damaged(target, attacker, amount), CreatureSlain(target, attacker).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:04Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:04Z"}

new_value: Created osric_combat.ttrpg with ascending AC attack resolution (BTHB tables, natural 1/20 rules), melee/missile/monster attack mechanics, charge action, surprise/initiative, Damaged/CreatureSlain events. Also added Large to Size enum in osric_core.

Created osric_conditions.ttrpg with 11 conditions: Prone, Stunned, Staggered (+attack_mod against bearer), Invisible (-4), Paralyzed/Sleeping (auto-hit), Fleeing/Surprised (bearer attacks suppressed), RearAttacked (+2), Concealed(level), Cover(penalty). Refactored combat mechanics to accept target: Character and attack_mod: int for condition modification.

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-xpp0","title":"OSRIC: Segment-based initiative system","description":"This is the most mechanically distinctive aspect of OSRIC combat and a key stress test for the DSL. OSRIC uses a 10-segment combat round with d6 group initiative (low roll = earlier segment). Key mechanics to model: (1) Surprise: d6 per side, result 1-2 = surprised for that many segments. DEX surprise modifier adjusts segments. (2) Initiative: d6 per side, determines starting segment for actions. Low is better. (3) Segment resolution: actions resolve segment-by-segment. Movement happens at 1/10 rate per segment. Spells have casting times in segments (begin at initiative segment, complete after casting time). (4) Spell interruption: if caster takes damage before spell completes, spell is lost. (5) Missile weapons with DEX bonus may fire in earlier segments. (6) Fighter multiple attacks happen at segments 1 and 10. (7) Weapon speed factor breaks ties when initiative is simultaneous. This system may require: tracking current segment, ordered action queues, interruptible casting. Investigate what DSL features are needed vs what exists — this is exploratory. Document any missing language features discovered.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:39Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:43:39Z"}

new_value: Segment-based initiative system complete: surprise, initiative rolls, segment assignment, fighter multi-attacks, spell timing, speed factor tie-breaking, movement, CastingSpell condition, SpellInterruption hook, charge interaction. Documented 4 DSL limitations.

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-9pc0","title":"Tag selector: skip functions with mismatched binding types instead of erroring","description":"When a condition uses [#attack_resolution](attacker: bearer) where bearer: Character, the checker errors because resolve_monster_attack has attacker: Monster. The selector should skip non-matching functions rather than failing. This forced Fleeing, Surprised, and CastingSpell in osric_conditions.ttrpg and osric_initiative.ttrpg to keep explicit dual modify clauses instead of collapsing to one tag selector. The correct behavior is: the selector simply doesn't fire for resolve_monster_attack since a Character condition shouldn't modify a Monster's attack as attacker.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:35Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:35Z"}

new_value: Binding types now narrow the selector match set instead of erroring. OSRIC conditions simplified to use tag selectors.

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-wmdw","title":"Add modulo operator (%)","description":"No modulo operator exists in the DSL. Needed for segment arithmetic in OSRIC's 10-segment initiative system (e.g., wrapping segment 11 back to 1), alternating round tracking (even/odd), and general game math. Currently noted as a discovered limitation in osric_initiative.ttrpg comments.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:35Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:35Z"}

new_value: Implemented % operator across full pipeline: lexer, AST, parser, checker, interpreter with tests

old_value: {"id":"tdsl-6kvo","title":"Allow entity keyword in tag selector bindings for polymorphic matching","description":"If tag selector bindings allowed attacker: entity (the polymorphic any-entity alias), a single modify clause could match both Character and Monster params. In osric_conditions.ttrpg, conditions like Fleeing and Surprised want to suppress all attacks by bearer regardless of whether the matched function takes Character or Monster as attacker. The condition body only uses result field overrides (result.outcome = AttackOutcome.Miss, result.damage = 0) which are entity-type-agnostic. This is an alternative or complement to the skip-mismatched-types approach in the other issue.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:35Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:22:35Z"}

new_value: Implemented: compute_common_params now unifies entity-typed params with different specific types to AnyEntity instead of dropping them. This allows conditions with 'on bearer: entity' to use tag selector bindings that match functions with different entity-typed params (e.g., attacker: Character and attacker: Monster).

new_value: {"description":"When DeductCost fires for a payer entity that has no provisioned budget, emit a diagnostic warning. With cost_payer in place, receiver-swap bugs no longer cause costs to charge the wrong entity (cost_payer always routes to the budgeted entity). However, calling a costed action entirely outside a with_budget scope (no budget provisioned at all) should produce a visible diagnostic. Free actions (cost free or cost-less) are legitimate and should NOT warn. The warning surfaces as a diagnostic effect (not an error), giving the host/CLI visibility without breaking execution. Budget enforcement via RequiresCheck (tdsl-h2im) separately causes the action to fail; this warning provides the \"why\" to help the author find the bug."}
