{"design":"# Charge / Set-vs-Charge + Round Tracking Design

## Overview

Full implementation of OSRIC 3.0 §1.6.2.1 (Charging Into Combat) and §1.6.2.10 (Set Weapon Against Charge), plus the general-purpose round/time tracking infrastructure needed to support it (and spell durations, memorization, etc.).

## Game Time Model

### Unified counter (segments as base unit)
A single monotonically increasing int representing game time in segments:
- 1 round = 10 segments
- 1 turn = 100 segments (10 rounds)
- 1 hour = 600 segments
- 1 day = 14,400 segments

Comparison and arithmetic are trivial int ops. Structured accessors (to_round, to_turn, etc.) provide readability.

### Surprise is off-clock
Surprise segments happen before the game clock starts. The surprise resolution code manages segment-scoped effects locally (knows exactly how many surprise segments there are). The game clock begins at round 1, segment 1 (counter value 0 or 1).

Effects that need to carry from surprise into combat get promoted to round-based durations by the surprise code.

### Duration enum (expanded)
```
enum Duration {
    end_of_round,
    segments(count: int),   // managed locally (surprise, casting), not via game clock
    rounds(count: int),
    turns(count: int),
    hours(count: int),
    days(count: int),
    indefinite
}
```

`segments(n)` exists as a variant but is handled by local code (surprise resolution, casting time tracking) rather than the game clock expiry system. All other timed variants map to segment counts for clock-based expiry.

### Duration to segments mapping
```
derive duration_to_segments(d: Duration) -> option<int> {
    match d {
        Duration.end_of_round => some(10),
        Duration.segments(n)  => some(n),       // only used by local code
        Duration.rounds(n)    => some(n * 10),
        Duration.turns(n)     => some(n * 100),
        Duration.hours(n)     => some(n * 600),
        Duration.days(n)      => some(n * 14400),
        Duration.indefinite   => none
    }
}
```

## Condition Expiry (DSL-side)

### Interpreter changes
- Add `applied_at: i64` field to `ActiveCondition` (Rust struct)
- Add `env.current_game_time: Option<i64>` to interpreter Env
- `apply_condition` stamps `applied_at` from `env.current_game_time`
- `to_value()` exposes `applied_at` as an int field to DSL code
- Host sets `env.current_game_time` before calling DSL functions

### DSL-side expiry function
```
function expire_conditions(game_time: int, combatants: list<Character>) {
    for c in combatants {
        for cond in conditions(c) {
            let limit = duration_to_segments(cond.duration)
            if limit.is_some() {
                let elapsed = game_time - cond.applied_at
                if elapsed >= limit.unwrap() {
                    remove_condition(c, cond)
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Round resolution integration
```
function resolve_round(
    game_time: int,          // NEW: current game time in segments
    round_number: int,       // NEW: for fighter attack alternation, cooldowns
    initiative: InitiativeResult,
    actions: list<RoundAction>
) {
    emit RoundStart(round: round_number, game_time: game_time)
    // ... existing segment loop (for seg in 1..=10) ...
    emit RoundEnd(round: round_number, game_time: game_time)
    // Expire timed conditions at round end
    // (combatant list derived from actions)
}
```

## Charge Rules (OSRIC 3.0 §1.6.2.1)

### What's needed
1. Weapon length data: add `length` field to MeleeWeaponDef (from Table 1.4.2.3c)
2. Charging condition: strips DEX AC bonus, -1 AC penalty if no DEX bonus
3. Charge cooldown: once per 10 rounds, tracked via `last_charge_time` entity field
4. Longer weapon first strike: if defender has longer weapon, attacks before charger
5. Encumbrance restriction: max encumbrance cannot charge unless mounted
6. Mounted lance double: lance + warhorse doubles weapon damage (not STR/magic/spec)

### Charge-vs-Set interaction (resolved in ChargeAction branch of resolve_action)
When ChargeAction resolves:
1. Apply Charging condition (loses DEX AC)
2. Check if target declared SetAgainstCharge in same round
3. If set: defender strikes first with 2x damage
4. If no set but defender has longer weapon: defender strikes first (normal damage)
5. If charger survives: resolve charge attack with +2 to-hit
6. Record last_charge_time for cooldown tracking

### Set-vs-Charge (§1.6.2.10)
- Set weapon readied at segment 1 (already implemented)
- Cannot attack unless actually charged
- Valid weapons: spear, javelin, lance (dismounted), pole arm, trident (already implemented)
- Double damage on hit against charger"}
old_value: {"id":"tdsl-ianf","title":"Charge and set-vs-charge rules are only partially implemented","description":"osric/osric_combat.ttrpg Charge action only applies +2 to hit; osric/osric_initiative.ttrpg has set-vs-charge as a multiplier derive but not full timing/interaction. OSRIC 3.0 sections 1.6.2.1 and 1.6.2.10 require: defender with longer weapon attacks first, charger loses DEX AC bonus, charge frequency limit (once per 10 rounds), and set weapon strike in segment 1 for double damage. Implement full charge/set interaction semantics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T03:51:02Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-03T03:51:02Z"}

{"design":"## Data Model

### New types (osric_core.ttrpg)

```
struct ClassLevel {
    class: Class
    level: int
    xp: int
}

enum ClassingMode { Single, Multi, Dual }
```

### Character entity changes

Remove:
- `class: Class`
- `level: int = 1`
- `xp: int = 0`

Add:
- `classes: list<ClassLevel>`
- `classing_mode: ClassingMode`

### Accessor conventions

- `first(char.classes).unwrap()` — primary/only class (Single), or first class (Multi/Dual original)
- `last(char.classes).unwrap()` — current class for Dual, same as first for Single
- `max([...])` / iteration — best-of aggregation for BTHB, saves

### Rule divergence by mode

| Mechanic | Single | Multi | Dual |
|---|---|---|---|
| HP | class HD | averaged across classes | summed |
| BTHB | class table | best across classes | best across classes |
| Saves | class table | best per category | best per category |
| Armour | class permission | most restrictive | current class only (while dormant) |
| XP gain | all to class | split evenly | all to current class |
| Abilities | all active | all active | old dormant until new level > old level |

### Dual-class dormancy

Derived, not stored: `classes[1].level <= classes[0].level` means original class is dormant. No extra field needed.

### Files requiring changes

1. **osric_core** — entity + new types
2. **osric_combat** — `effective_target_ac` Monk checks, `resolve_melee/missile_attack` BTHB + Monk damage (use best_bthb, check any class == Monk)
3. **osric_saves** — `saving_throws_for` → aggregate best per category
4. **osric_character** — `prime_req_xp_bonus` → per-class or primary class
5. **osric_class** — `check_level_up` → per ClassLevel entry
6. **osric_initiative** — `has_fighter_multi_attack` → check any fighter-group class

### New aggregation derives needed

- `best_bthb(char) -> int` — max BTHB across all classes
- `best_saves(char) -> SavingThrows` — best per save category
- `effective_armour_permission(char) -> ArmourPermission` — mode-dependent
- `is_class(char, class) -> bool` — true if any ClassLevel matches (replaces `char.class == X`)
- `class_level(char, class) -> option<int>` — level for a specific class if held
- `has_active_class(char, class) -> bool` — like is_class but respects dual-class dormancy
- `primary_class(char) -> ClassLevel` — first entry (original/only)
- `current_class(char) -> ClassLevel` — last entry (active for dual, same as primary for single/multi)"}
old_value: {"id":"tdsl-l7fc","title":"Design: multi-class data model for Character entity","description":"Character currently has class: Class (single) and level: int (single). Multi-classing needs 2-3 simultaneous classes, dual-classing needs old+new class with level tracking. Proposed: change to classes: map\u003cClass, int\u003e mapping class to level. All derives that use class/level (bthb, saving_throws_for, xp_for_level, fighter_attacks_per_round, spell slots, etc.) must iterate over all held classes and pick best or aggregate. Consider: primary_class field for XP allocation, active_class for dual-classing restrictions, HP calculation (roll per class, divide by num classes). This touches osric_core, osric_class, osric_combat, osric_saves, osric_character, osric_initiative.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tfgast@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T06:00:19Z","created_by":"Theodore Gast","updated_at":"2026-03-04T06:00:19Z"}
